verses.meaning_en: Chapter 17 opens with a question that is both philosophically sophisticated and practically urgent: Arjuna has heard in Chapter 16 that acting against scriptural injunction leads to no perfection, no happiness, no liberation. But he has also observed in the world people who sincerely, ardently, devotionally worship — outside any scriptural framework, following only their own inner impulse and faith. What is the status of their faith? Where does it place them? The question turns on a profound tension: between the authority of tradition (shastra) and the authority of inner sincerity (shraddha). Krishna's answer in Chapter 17 is a complete taxonomy of faith — its three forms, and how those three forms ramify through every domain of human life: worship, food, sacrifice, austerity, charity, and speech.
verses.meaning_hi: अध्याय 17 एक ऐसे प्रश्न से खुलता है जो दार्शनिक रूप से परिष्कृत और व्यावहारिक रूप से तत्काल है: अर्जुन ने अध्याय 16 में सुना कि शास्त्रविधि के विरुद्ध आचरण से न सिद्धि, न सुख, न मुक्ति मिलती है। किंतु उसने ऐसे लोग भी देखे हैं जो श्रद्धापूर्वक, उत्साहपूर्वक, भक्तिपूर्वक पूजा करते हैं — किसी शास्त्रीय ढाँचे के बाहर, केवल अपनी आंतरिक आस्था के अनुसार। उनकी श्रद्धा की स्थिति क्या है? प्रश्न एक गहरे तनाव पर टिका है: परंपरा के प्राधिकार (शास्त्र) और आंतरिक ईमानदारी के प्राधिकार (श्रद्धा) के बीच।

verses.sanskrit:
श्रीभगवानुवाच |
त्रिविधा भवति श्रद्धा देहिनां सा स्वभावजा |
सात्त्विकी राजसी चैव तामसी चेति तां शृणु ||२||
verses.translation_en: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: According to the modes of nature acquired by the embodied soul, one's faith can be of three kinds — in goodness, in passion, or in ignorance. Now hear about these.
verses.translation_hi: श्रीभगवान बोले: देहियों की स्वभावजा श्रद्धा तीन प्रकार की होती है — सात्त्विकी, राजसी और तामसी। उसे सुनो।
verses.meaning_en: Krishna's answer begins with a foundational insight: shraddha (faith) is not one thing but three, and its variety arises from the gunas — from the temperament one has developed through accumulated karma and choice. 'Svabhava-ja' — born of one's own nature, one's svabhava. Faith is not externally assigned but is the natural orientation of the soul given its current guna-composition. This is simultaneously a liberating and a sobering truth: liberating because it means faith is genuine regardless of its form; sobering because it means the quality of one's faith is a direct reflection of where one stands in the inner life. The entire chapter unfolds from this single premise: the guna that dominates one's nature shapes the faith one has, and that faith in turn shapes every act of devotion, every choice of food, every sacrifice, every practice of austerity.
verses.meaning_hi: कृष्ण का उत्तर एक मूलभूत अंतर्दृष्टि से शुरू होता है: श्रद्धा एक नहीं बल्कि तीन प्रकार की है, और इसकी विविधता गुणों से — संचित कर्म और चुनाव से विकसित स्वभाव से — उत्पन्न होती है। 'स्वभावजा' — अपनी प्रकृति से जन्मी। श्रद्धा बाहर से निर्धारित नहीं बल्कि आत्मा की वर्तमान गुण-संरचना के अनुसार स्वाभाविक अभिमुखता है। यह एक साथ मुक्तिदायी और गंभीर सत्य है: मुक्तिदायी क्योंकि श्रद्धा अपने रूप में वास्तविक है; गंभीर क्योंकि इसकी गुणवत्ता आंतरिक जीवन की प्रत्यक्ष झलक है।

verses.sanskrit:
सत्त्वानुरूपा सर्वस्य श्रद्धा भवति भारत |
श्रद्धामयोऽयं पुरुषो यो यच्छ्रद्धः स एव सः ||३||
verses.translation_en: O son of Bharata, according to one's existence under the various modes of nature, one evolves a particular kind of faith. The living being is said to be of a particular faith according to the modes he has acquired.
verses.translation_hi: हे भारत! सभी की श्रद्धा उनके सत्त्व (स्वभाव) के अनुरूप होती है। यह पुरुष श्रद्धामय है — जो जिस श्रद्धा का है वही वह है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Sattvanurupa sarvasya shraddha bhavati Bharata' — the faith of each person corresponds to their sattva, their inner nature. And then one of the most psychologically penetrating statements in the entire Gita: 'shraddha-mayo'yam purusho yo yac-chraddhah sa eva sah' — this person is made of faith; as is one's faith, so is one. Faith here is not a religious belief held intellectually but the deep orientation of the whole person: what one truly values, what one ultimately trusts, what one gives oneself to. This orientation shapes not just what one worships but what one eats, how one speaks, how one spends energy, and what one considers worth sacrificing for. The person IS their shraddha — identity and faith are not separate.
verses.meaning_hi: 'सत्त्वानुरूपा सर्वस्य श्रद्धा भवति भारत' — सभी की श्रद्धा उनकी आंतरिक प्रकृति के अनुरूप। और फिर गीता की सबसे मनोवैज्ञानिक रूप से गहरी उक्तियों में से एक: 'श्रद्धामयोऽयं पुरुषः — यो यच्छ्रद्धः स एव सः' — यह पुरुष श्रद्धामय है; जो जिस श्रद्धा का है वही वह है। श्रद्धा यहाँ बौद्धिक धार्मिक विश्वास नहीं बल्कि संपूर्ण व्यक्ति की गहरी अभिमुखता है: जिसे कोई सच में महत्व देता है, जिस पर अंततः भरोसा करता है। व्यक्ति अपनी श्रद्धा ही है।

verses.sanskrit:
यजन्ते सात्त्विका देवान्यक्षरक्षांसि राजसाः |
प्रेतान्भूतगणांश्चान्ये यजन्ते तामसा जनाः ||४||
verses.translation_en: Men in the mode of goodness worship the demigods; those in the mode of passion worship the demons; and those in the mode of ignorance worship ghosts and spirits.
verses.translation_hi: सात्त्विक लोग देवताओं को, राजसिक यक्ष-राक्षसों को और तामसिक जन प्रेत-भूतगणों को पूजते हैं।
verses.meaning_en: The three kinds of faith immediately differentiate their objects of worship: sattvic people worship the devas — the celestial beings associated with light, order, and the higher functions of the cosmos. Rajasic people worship yakshas and rakshasas — beings of power, wealth, and will, who embody the rajasic qualities of dominance, accumulation, and force. Tamasic people worship pretas (ghosts) and bhutaganas (groups of spirits) — beings associated with the lower realms, with death and darkness. The object of worship is a mirror of the worshipper: one is drawn to what one resembles. Sattvic consciousness naturally gravitates toward clarity and elevation; rajasic toward power and acquisition; tamasic toward the obscure, the morbid, and the unconscious.
verses.meaning_hi: तीन प्रकार की श्रद्धा तुरंत अपनी पूजा की वस्तुओं को विभेदित करती है: सात्त्विक देवताओं को — प्रकाश, व्यवस्था से जुड़े दिव्य प्राणी। राजसिक यक्ष-राक्षसों को — शक्ति, धन, प्रभुत्व के प्राणी। तामसिक प्रेत-भूतगणों को — निम्न लोकों, मृत्यु और अंधकार से जुड़े। पूजा की वस्तु पूजक का दर्पण है: जिससे कोई मिलता-जुलता है उसकी ओर आकर्षित होता है।

verses.sanskrit:
अशास्त्रविहितं घोरं तप्यन्ते ये तपो जनाः |
दम्भाहङ्कारसंयुक्ताः कामरागबलान्विताः ||५||
verses.translation_en: Those who undergo severe austerities and penances not recommended in the scriptures, performing them out of pride and egoism, who are impelled by lust and attachment —
verses.translation_hi: जो लोग शास्त्रविहित नहीं ऐसे घोर तप करते हैं, दम्भ और अहंकार से युक्त, काम और राग से प्रेरित —
verses.meaning_en: Verses 5 and 6 together describe a dangerous form of austerity that stands outside all three guna-categories as a warning: the practice of extreme, self-invented austerities driven by pride and desire rather than by genuine spiritual aspiration. 'Ashaastra-vihitam ghoram' — terrible austerities not sanctioned by scripture. 'Dambha-ahankara-samyuktah' — joined with ostentation (dambha) and ego (ahankara). 'Kama-raga-balanvitah' — propelled by the force of desire (kama) and attachment (raga). The combination is telling: extreme physical mortification + ego-inflation + desire. These practices are not born from wisdom or genuine renunciation but from the ego's need to be seen as exceptional, as supremely spiritual, as beyond ordinary human limitation — the ego devouring itself in the name of transcendence.
verses.meaning_hi: श्लोक 5 और 6 मिलकर एक खतरनाक प्रकार की तपस्या का वर्णन करते हैं: घमंड और इच्छा से प्रेरित अत्यंत, स्व-आविष्कृत तपस्या। 'अशास्त्रविहितं घोरम्' — शास्त्र द्वारा अनुमोदित नहीं ऐसी भयंकर तपस्या। 'दम्भाहंकारसंयुक्ताः' — दम्भ और अहंकार से युक्त। 'कामरागबलान्विताः' — काम और राग की शक्ति से प्रेरित। संयोजन बताने वाला है: चरम शारीरिक तपस्या + अहंकार-फुलाव + इच्छा।

verses.sanskrit:
कर्शयन्तः शरीरस्थं भूतग्राममचेतसः |
मां चैवान्तःशरीरस्थं तान्विद्ध्यासुरनिश्चयान् ||६||
verses.translation_en: Those who are possessed of a demoniac determination and who torture the material elements of the body as well as the Supersoul dwelling within are to be known as demons.
verses.translation_hi: शरीरस्थ भूतग्राम को और शरीर के भीतर स्थित मुझे भी कर्शित करने वाले अचेतस लोगों को आसुर निश्चय वाले जानो।
verses.meaning_en: 'Karshayantah sharira-stham bhuta-gramam acetasah' — heedlessly tormenting the aggregate of elements within the body. 'Mam caivantah sharira-stham' — and also Me, who dwell within the body. This is the profound completion of the warning: extreme, ego-driven austerity does not merely harm the body but torments the Divine presence within it. The Paramatma, seated in the heart of every being, is disturbed by the violence these practitioners direct at their own bodies. 'Tan viddhy asura-nishcayan' — know them to be of demonic resolve. The message is clear: genuine austerity honors the body as the temple of the Divine; the demonic perversion of austerity treats the body as an enemy to be punished, and in doing so, punishes the Divine that inhabits it.
verses.meaning_hi: 'शरीरस्थं भूतग्राममचेतसः' — शरीर के भूत-समूह को बेपरवाही से पीड़ित करना। 'मां चैवान्तःशरीरस्थम्' — और मुझे भी जो शरीर के भीतर हूँ। यह चेतावनी की गहरी परिपूर्णता है: अहंकार-चालित तपस्या न केवल शरीर को हानि पहुँचाती है बल्कि उसके भीतर दिव्य उपस्थिति को पीड़ित करती है। 'आसुरनिश्चयान्' — आसुरी संकल्प वाले।

verses.sanskrit:
आहारस्त्वपि सर्वस्य त्रिविधो भवति प्रियः |
यज्ञस्तपस्तथा दानं तेषां भेदमिमं शृणु ||७||
verses.translation_en: Even the food each person prefers is of three kinds, according to the three modes of material nature. The same is true of sacrifices, austerities, and charity. Now hear of the distinctions between them.
verses.translation_hi: सभी को प्रिय आहार भी तीन प्रकार का होता है — और यज्ञ, तप और दान भी। उनके भेद को सुनो।
verses.meaning_en: Krishna now extends the taxonomy of faith into four concrete domains of daily life: ahara (food), yajna (sacrifice), tapas (austerity), and dana (charity). This is a remarkable move: the abstract teaching of the three gunas is made palpably practical. What you eat, how you worship, what you endure, and what you give — all of these are expressions of the faith that constitutes your identity. Nothing is neutral. Every choice of nourishment, every act of ritual, every form of self-discipline, every gesture of generosity carries the signature of the guna that dominates the faith behind it. The teaching makes the inner life visible through its outer expressions.
verses.meaning_hi: कृष्ण अब श्रद्धा की वर्गीकरण को दैनिक जीवन के चार ठोस क्षेत्रों में विस्तारित करते हैं: आहार, यज्ञ, तप और दान। यह एक उल्लेखनीय कदम है: तीन गुणों की अमूर्त शिक्षा को व्यावहारिक रूप से स्पष्ट किया जाता है। तुम क्या खाते हो, कैसे पूजते हो, क्या सहन करते हो और क्या देते हो — ये सभी उस श्रद्धा की अभिव्यक्तियाँ हैं जो तुम्हारी पहचान बनाती है। कुछ भी तटस्थ नहीं।

verses.sanskrit:
आयुःसत्त्वबलारोग्यसुखप्रीतिविवर्धनाः |
रस्याः स्निग्धाः स्थिरा हृद्या आहाराः सात्त्विकप्रियाः ||८||
verses.translation_en: Foods dear to those in the mode of goodness increase the duration of life, purify one's existence, and give strength, health, happiness, and satisfaction. Such foods are juicy, fatty, wholesome, and pleasing to the heart.
verses.translation_hi: आयु, सत्त्व, बल, आरोग्य, सुख और प्रीति बढ़ाने वाले — रस्य, स्निग्ध, स्थिर और हृद्य आहार सात्त्विकप्रिय हैं।
verses.meaning_en: The sattvic diet is described by its effects and its qualities: effects — 'ayuh-sattva-bala-arogya-sukha-priti-vivardhanah' — it increases lifespan (ayuh), vitality/inner purity (sattva), strength (bala), health (arogya), happiness (sukha), and delight (priti). Six benefits, all oriented toward fullness of life. Qualities — 'rasyah' (juicy, flavorful — full of rasa, the life-essence), 'snigdhah' (oily, unctuous — the foods that nourish and lubricate), 'sthira' (sustaining, stabilizing — foods that give lasting energy rather than quick spikes), 'hridyah' (pleasing to the heart — agreeable, not harsh). The sattvic diet is not ascetic or joyless; it is nourishing, delicious, and sustaining — a diet that honors the body as the vehicle of consciousness.
verses.meaning_hi: सात्त्विक आहार को इसके प्रभावों और गुणों से वर्णित: प्रभाव — 'आयुःसत्त्वबलारोग्यसुखप्रीतिविवर्धनाः' — आयु, सत्त्व, बल, आरोग्य, सुख और प्रीति बढ़ाना। छह लाभ, सभी जीवन की पूर्णता की ओर। गुण — रस्य (रसपूर्ण), स्निग्ध (चिकना, पोषक), स्थिर (स्थायी ऊर्जा देने वाला), हृद्य (हृदय को प्रिय)। सात्त्विक आहार तपस्वी या आनंदहीन नहीं; यह पोषक, स्वादिष्ट और टिकाऊ है।

verses.sanskrit:
कट्वम्ललवणात्युष्णतीक्ष्णरूक्षविदाहिनः |
आहारा राजसस्येष्टा दुःखशोकामयप्रदाः ||९||
verses.translation_en: Foods that are too bitter, too sour, salty, hot, pungent, dry, and burning are dear to those in the mode of passion. Such foods cause distress, misery, and disease.
verses.translation_hi: कडुए, खट्टे, नमकीन, अत्यंत उष्ण, तीखे, रूखे और जलनयुक्त आहार राजसिक को प्रिय हैं — वे दुःख, शोक और रोग देते हैं।
verses.meaning_en: 'Katv-amla-lavana-atyushna-tikshna-ruksha-vidahinah' — bitter, sour, salty, excessively hot, pungent, dry, and burning. These six qualities together describe food that agitates the system: stimulating, sharp, inflaming. 'Rajasasya ishtah' — dear to the rajasic person. The rajasic person is drawn to food that excites, stimulates, and provides the sensation of intensity — food as entertainment, as stimulation, as the amplification of desire. 'Duhkha-shoka-amaya-pradah' — such food produces duhkha (suffering), shoka (grief), and amaya (disease). The trajectory is direct: the food that excites the senses also excites the mind, and an excited mind is a suffering mind — prone to craving, frustration, and the diseases that arise from chronic agitation.
verses.meaning_hi: 'कट्वम्ललवणात्युष्णतीक्ष्णरूक्षविदाहिनः' — कडुए, खट्टे, नमकीन, अत्यंत उष्ण, तीखे, रूखे और जलन वाले। ये छह गुण मिलकर वह भोजन बताते हैं जो प्रणाली को उत्तेजित करता है: उत्तेजक, तीखा, भड़काने वाला। 'दुःखशोकामयप्रदाः' — ऐसा भोजन दुःख, शोक और रोग उत्पन्न करता है। दिशा सीधी है: जो भोजन इन्द्रियों को उत्तेजित करता है वह मन को भी उत्तेजित करता है, और उत्तेजित मन पीड़ित मन है।

verses.sanskrit:
यातयामं गतरसं पूति पर्युषितं च यत् |
उच्छिष्टमपि चामेध्यं भोजनं तामसप्रियम् ||१०||
verses.translation_en: Food prepared more than three hours before being eaten, food that is tasteless, decomposed, and putrid, and food consisting of remnants and untouchable things is dear to those in the mode of darkness.
verses.translation_hi: यातयाम (तीन घंटे से अधिक पुराना), गतरस, पूति (सड़ा), पर्युषित (बासी), उच्छिष्ट और अमेध्य भोजन तामसिक को प्रिय है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Yata-yamam' — literally 'gone three hours' — stale food that has lost its vital energy (prana). 'Gata-rasam' — tasteless, devoid of essence. 'Puti' — rotten, putrid. 'Paryushitam' — spoiled, kept too long. 'Ucchishtam' — remnants of food left by others. 'Amedhyam' — ritually impure, untouchable. The tamasic person's relationship with food is characterized by indifference to its quality — the dullness of tamas extends to the absence of discrimination about what enters the body. Just as tamas in the mind produces heaviness, dullness, and the obscuring of awareness, tamasic food produces the same effects: it weighs down the system, clouds the mind, and reinforces the very inertia that makes spiritual practice impossible.
verses.meaning_hi: 'यातयामम्' — तीन घंटे से अधिक पुराना, प्राण-रहित। 'गतरसम्' — स्वादहीन, सारहीन। 'पूति' — सड़ा। 'पर्युषितम्' — बासी। 'उच्छिष्टम्' — जूठन। 'अमेध्यम्' — अशुद्ध। भोजन के प्रति तामसिक व्यक्ति का संबंध इसकी गुणवत्ता के प्रति उदासीनता से चिह्नित है। जैसे तमस मन में भारीपन और जागरूकता को आच्छादित करता है, तामसिक भोजन वही प्रभाव उत्पन्न करता है।

verses.sanskrit:
अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्यज्ञो विधिदृष्टो य इज्यते |
यष्टव्यमेवेति मनः समाधाय स सात्त्विकः ||११||
verses.translation_en: Of sacrifices, the sacrifice performed according to the directions of scripture, as a matter of duty, by those who desire no reward, is of the nature of goodness.
verses.translation_hi: जो यज्ञ फलाकांक्षा रहित होकर शास्त्रविधि से, 'यज्ञ करना ही है' — ऐसा मन में समाधान करके किया जाता है, वह सात्त्विक है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Aphala-akankshi-bhir yajno vidhi-drishto ya ijyate' — sacrifice performed according to scriptural direction by those who have no desire for its fruits. 'Yashtavyam eveti manah samadhaya' — with the mind settled in the understanding that it simply must be done — that sacrifice is one's duty, one's offering, the right thing to do, regardless of personal benefit. 'Sa sattvikah' — that is sattvic. The defining quality of sattvic sacrifice is not the elaborate ritual form but the inner disposition: the absence of the calculative relationship with the Divine, the absence of spiritual transaction ('I give this, therefore give me that'). Sattvic sacrifice is pure offering — the self given to the Divine without expectation of return, because the offering itself is the reward.
verses.meaning_hi: 'अफलाकांक्षिभिर्यज्ञो विधिदृष्टो य इज्यते' — फलाकांक्षा रहित होकर शास्त्रविधि से किया यज्ञ। 'यष्टव्यमेवेति मनः समाधाय' — मन में यह समाधान करके कि यज्ञ करना ही है। सात्त्विक यज्ञ की परिभाषित गुणवत्ता विस्तृत अनुष्ठान रूप नहीं बल्कि आंतरिक भाव है: दिव्य के साथ गणनात्मक संबंध का अभाव। सात्त्विक यज्ञ शुद्ध अर्पण है।

verses.sanskrit:
अभिसन्धाय तु फलं दम्भार्थमपि चैव यत् |
इज्यते भरतश्रेष्ठ तं यज्ञं विद्धि राजसम् ||१२||
verses.translation_en: But the sacrifice performed for some material benefit, or for the sake of pride, O chief of the Bharatas, you should know to be in the mode of passion.
verses.translation_hi: हे भरतश्रेष्ठ! किंतु जो यज्ञ फल की अभीप्सा रखकर और दम्भ के लिए किया जाता है — उस यज्ञ को राजसिक जानो।
verses.meaning_en: 'Abhisandhaya tu phalam' — but with an eye toward the fruit, the result. 'Dambhartham api caiva' — and even for the sake of show, of reputation. 'Yat ijyate... tam yajnam viddhi rajasam' — what is worshipped thus — know that sacrifice to be rajasic. Rajasic sacrifice is the sacrifice of transaction and performance: it is offered with the hand open to receive something in return — material benefit, social prestige, divine favor, the reputation for piety. The form may be impeccable — the correct procedures followed, the correct mantras recited — but the inner motivation is the calculation of the ego. Rajasic sacrifice does not dissolve the self; it employs the sacred as a tool for the self's advancement.
verses.meaning_hi: 'अभिसन्धाय तु फलम्' — फल की दृष्टि रखकर। 'दम्भार्थमपि चैव' — और दम्भ के लिए भी। राजसिक यज्ञ लेन-देन और प्रदर्शन का यज्ञ है: यह कुछ वापस पाने के लिए हाथ खुला रखकर अर्पित किया जाता है — भौतिक लाभ, सामाजिक प्रतिष्ठा, दिव्य कृपा। रूप त्रुटिहीन हो सकता है, किंतु आंतरिक प्रेरणा अहंकार की गणना है।

verses.sanskrit:
विधिहीनमसृष्टान्नं मन्त्रहीनमदक्षिणम् |
श्रद्धाविरहितं यज्ञं तामसं परिचक्षते ||१३||
verses.translation_en: Any sacrifice performed without regard for the directions of scripture, without distribution of prasadam, without chanting of Vedic hymns, without recompense to the priests, and without faith is considered to be in the mode of ignorance.
verses.translation_hi: विधिहीन, अन्नवितरणरहित, मन्त्रहीन, दक्षिणारहित और श्रद्धाविरहित यज्ञ तामसिक कहा जाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Vidhi-hinam' — without the proper method (the organized, living wisdom of how sacrifice is to be conducted). 'Asrishta-annam' — without the distribution of food (the giving that is central to authentic sacrifice). 'Mantra-hinam' — without sacred sound, without the vibrations that align the sacrifice with the cosmic order. 'Adakshinam' — without honorarium to the officiating priests (the absence of the giving-back that completes the circuit of the offering). 'Shraddha-virahitam' — devoid of faith, the foundational inner quality. Tamasic sacrifice is sacrifice stripped of everything essential: no understanding, no giving, no sacred sound, no gratitude, no faith. It is the shell of ritual from which the soul has entirely departed — performed perhaps from habit, or compulsion, or dark purpose.
verses.meaning_hi: 'विधिहीनम्' — उचित विधि के बिना। 'असृष्टान्नम्' — अन्नवितरण के बिना। 'मन्त्रहीनम्' — पवित्र ध्वनि के बिना। 'अदक्षिणम्' — दक्षिणा के बिना। 'श्रद्धाविरहितम्' — श्रद्धारहित। तामसिक यज्ञ हर आवश्यक चीज से वंचित यज्ञ है: कोई समझ नहीं, कोई दान नहीं, कोई पवित्र ध्वनि नहीं, कोई श्रद्धा नहीं। यह अनुष्ठान का खोल है जिसकी आत्मा जा चुकी है।

verses.sanskrit:
देवद्विजगुरुप्राज्ञपूजनं शौचमार्जवम् |
ब्रह्मचर्यमहिंसा च शारीरं तप उच्यते ||१४||
verses.translation_en: Austerity of the body consists in worship of the Supreme Lord, the brahmanas, the spiritual master, and superiors like the father and mother, and in cleanliness, simplicity, celibacy, and nonviolence.
verses.translation_hi: देव, द्विज, गुरु और प्राज्ञ की पूजा, शौच, आर्जव, ब्रह्मचर्य और अहिंसा — शारीरिक तप कहलाता है।
verses.meaning_en: Verses 14, 15, and 16 offer a remarkable three-part analysis of tapas (austerity) that divides it by the dimension of the person it purifies: body, speech, and mind. The austerity of the body begins, surprisingly, not with physical hardship but with veneration: 'deva-dvija-guru-prajna-pujanam' — worship of the Divine, the brahman-teachers, the spiritual master (guru), and the learned (prajna). Then: 'shaucam' (cleanliness of body), 'arjavam' (straightforwardness — the physical posture and movement of the honest person), 'brahmacarya' (continence — the channeling of vital energy toward spiritual ends), and 'ahimsa' (non-violence in bodily conduct). The austerity of the body is not self-punishment but the disciplined consecration of physical life.
verses.meaning_hi: श्लोक 14, 15 और 16 तपस का एक उल्लेखनीय त्रिखंडीय विश्लेषण प्रस्तुत करते हैं — व्यक्ति के उस आयाम के अनुसार जिसे यह शुद्ध करता है: शरीर, वाणी और मन। शारीरिक तपस आश्चर्यजनक रूप से शारीरिक कठिनाई से नहीं बल्कि सम्मान से शुरू होता है: देव, द्विज, गुरु और प्राज्ञ की पूजा। फिर: शौच, आर्जव, ब्रह्मचर्य और अहिंसा। शरीर का तप आत्म-दंड नहीं बल्कि भौतिक जीवन का अनुशासित समर्पण है।

verses.sanskrit:
अनुद्वेगकरं वाक्यं सत्यं प्रियहितं च यत् |
स्वाध्यायाभ्यसनं चैव वाङ्मयं तप उच्यते ||१५||
verses.translation_en: Austerity of speech consists in speaking words that are truthful, pleasing, beneficial, and not agitating to others, and also in regularly reciting Vedic literature.
verses.translation_hi: अनुद्वेगकर, सत्य, प्रिय और हितकर वाक्य बोलना तथा स्वाध्याय का अभ्यास — वाङ्मय तप कहलाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Anudvega-karam vakyam' — speech that does not agitate, does not disturb, does not injure. 'Satyam' — truthful. 'Priya' — pleasing, kind. 'Hitam' — beneficial, genuinely good for the listener. 'Svaadhyayabhyasanam' — regular practice of self-study, of reciting and studying the sacred texts. Four qualities of sattvic speech: non-agitating, true, kind, and beneficial — plus the fifth of ongoing scriptural recitation. The sequence is instructive: the first quality is negative (not agitating), the next two are positive qualities of content (true and kind), the fourth speaks to purpose (beneficial), and the fifth to discipline (self-study). Genuine austerity of speech is not silence alone but the disciplined cultivation of words that serve both truth and the well-being of all who hear them.
verses.meaning_hi: 'अनुद्वेगकरं वाक्यम्' — न उत्तेजित करने वाला, न क्षुब्ध करने वाला, न घायल करने वाला। 'सत्यम्' — सत्य। 'प्रियम्' — प्रिय, दयालु। 'हितम्' — सुनने वाले के लिए वास्तव में भला। 'स्वाध्यायाभ्यसनम्' — स्वाध्याय का नियमित अभ्यास। सात्त्विक वाणी के चार गुण: अनुत्तेजक, सत्य, प्रिय और हितकर — साथ ही स्वाध्याय का पाँचवाँ अनुशासन।

verses.sanskrit:
मनःप्रसादः सौम्यत्वं मौनमात्मविनिग्रहः |
भावसंशुद्धिरित्येतत्तपो मानसमुच्यते ||१६||
verses.translation_en: And satisfaction, simplicity, gravity, self-control, and purification of one's existence are the austerities of the mind.
verses.translation_hi: मनःप्रसाद (मन की प्रसन्नता), सौम्यता, मौन, आत्मविनिग्रह और भावसंशुद्धि — यह मानसिक तप कहलाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Manah-prasadah' — the cheerfulness of mind, its settled serenity — the natural luminosity of a mind that has been cleared of its agitations. 'Saumyatvam' — gentleness, benevolence — the mind's quality of soft, compassionate engagement with the world. 'Maunam' — silence — not merely the absence of speech but the inner silence of a mind that has stopped its compulsive narration of experience. 'Atma-vinigrahah' — self-control — the mastery of the mental movements, the ability to direct attention rather than be carried by it. 'Bhava-samshuddhih' — purification of being, purity of intention — the transparency of motive that leaves no hidden agenda. These five qualities together constitute the austerity of the mind: not forced restriction but the natural clarity that comes from genuine inner work.
verses.meaning_hi: 'मनःप्रसादः' — मन की प्रसन्नता, उसकी स्थिर शांति। 'सौम्यत्वम्' — कोमलता, करुणापूर्ण सगाई। 'मौनम्' — मौन — न केवल वाणी का अभाव बल्कि मन की बाध्यकारी कथा का आंतरिक मौन। 'आत्मविनिग्रहः' — आत्म-नियंत्रण। 'भावसंशुद्धिः' — भाव की शुद्धि, इरादे की पारदर्शिता। ये पाँच गुण मिलकर मानसिक तप का गठन करते हैं।

verses.sanskrit:
श्रद्धया परया तप्तं तपस्तत्त्रिविधं नरैः |
अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्युक्तैः सात्त्विकं परिचक्षते ||१७||
verses.translation_en: This threefold austerity, practiced with transcendental faith by men not expecting material benefits but engaged in the service of the Supreme, is called austerity in goodness.
verses.translation_hi: फलाकांक्षारहित युक्त पुरुषों द्वारा परम श्रद्धा से तपाया गया यह त्रिविध तप सात्त्विक कहा जाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Shraddha-ya paraya taptam' — undergone with supreme (para) faith, the highest quality of shraddha. 'Tapas tat tri-vidham naraih' — this threefold austerity (of body, speech, and mind as described in verses 14-16) by human beings. 'Aphala-akankshi-bhir yuktaih' — by those who are connected (yukta — in yoga) and free from desire for fruits. 'Sattvikam paricakshate' — is called sattvic. The sattvic quality of the austerity is determined not by the external rigor of the practices but by two inner qualities: the height of the faith (para-shraddha — the supreme, unconditional faith) and the absence of expectation of return. When austerity is practiced from these two foundations, it purifies; when it is practiced from any other motive, it binds.
verses.meaning_hi: 'श्रद्धया परया तप्तम्' — परम श्रद्धा से तपाया गया। 'अफलाकांक्षिभिर्युक्तैः' — फलाकांक्षा रहित युक्त पुरुषों द्वारा। तपस की सात्त्विक गुणवत्ता बाहरी कठोरता से नहीं बल्कि दो आंतरिक गुणों से निर्धारित होती है: श्रद्धा की ऊँचाई और वापसी की अपेक्षा का अभाव। जब तपस इन दो आधारों से किया जाता है तो शुद्ध करता है; किसी अन्य प्रेरणा से बाँधता है।

verses.sanskrit:
सत्कारमानपूजार्थं तपो दम्भेन चैव यत् |
क्रियते तदिह प्रोक्तं राजसं चलमध्रुवम् ||१८||
verses.translation_en: Austerities performed out of pride and for the sake of gaining respect, honor, and worship are said to be in the mode of passion. They are neither stable nor permanent.
verses.translation_hi: सत्कार, मान और पूजा के लिए तथा दम्भ से किया गया तप — यहाँ राजसिक, चल और अध्रुव कहा जाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Satkara-mana-pujartham tapah dambhena caiva yat' — austerity done for the purpose of receiving honor (satkara), respect (mana), and worship (puja), and done with ostentation (dambha). 'Tad iha proktam rajasam' — that here is called rajasic. 'Calam adhruvam' — unstable (cala — moving, shaky) and impermanent (adhruvam — not fixed, not lasting). The rajasic austerity is particularly poignant in its self-defeating nature: the person undergoes genuine hardship, genuine discipline — but the entire structure is built on the desire for recognition. And since it is 'cala-adhruvam' — unstable and impermanent — it cannot provide what it promises. The praise comes and goes; the recognition is never enough; the spiritual fruit never ripens because the root of the practice is in the ego's hunger rather than in genuine transformation.
verses.meaning_hi: 'सत्कारमानपूजार्थं तपो दम्भेन चैव' — सत्कार, मान और पूजा के लिए, दम्भ से किया। 'चलमध्रुवम्' — अस्थिर और अनित्य। राजसिक तपस की प्रकृति स्व-पराजयकारी है: व्यक्ति वास्तविक कठिनाई सहता है — किंतु पूरी संरचना मान्यता की इच्छा पर बनी है। और चूँकि यह 'चल-अध्रुव' है, यह वह नहीं दे सकता जो वादा करता है।

verses.sanskrit:
मूढग्राहेणात्मनो यत्पीडया क्रियते तपः |
परस्योत्सादनार्थं वा तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ||१९||
verses.translation_en: And austerity performed out of foolishness, with self-torture, or to destroy or injure others, is said to be in the mode of ignorance.
verses.translation_hi: जो तप मूढ़ग्राह से, स्वयं को पीड़ित करके, या दूसरों के उत्सादन (नाश) के लिए किया जाता है — वह तामसिक कहा जाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Mudha-grahena atmano yat pidaya kriyate tapah' — austerity performed out of foolish obstinacy (mudha-graha — the pig-headed clinging of ignorance), with the infliction of pain upon oneself. 'Parasyotsadanartham va' — or for the purpose of causing harm (utsadana — destruction, ruination) to another. 'Tat tamasam udahritam' — that is called tamasic. Tamasic austerity moves in two directions: inward (self-torture born of delusion, the violent ego-driven mortification already warned against in verses 5-6) and outward (practices undertaken to harm or destroy an enemy — the dark tantric uses of austerity as a weapon). Both directions are rooted in the same quality: mudha-graha — the stupid, obstinate clinging of tamas that cannot distinguish between genuine purification and self-destruction.
verses.meaning_hi: 'मूढग्राहेणात्मनो यत्पीडया क्रियते तपः' — मूढ़ग्राह से, स्वयं को पीड़ित करके किया तप। 'परस्योत्सादनार्थं वा' — या दूसरे के नाश के लिए। तामसिक तपस दो दिशाओं में चलता है: अंतर्मुखी (भ्रम से उत्पन्न आत्म-यातना) और बाह्यमुखी (शत्रु को हानि पहुँचाने के लिए तपस)। दोनों एक ही गुण में जड़ें जमाते हैं: मूढ़ग्राह — तमस की मूर्खतापूर्ण, हठी पकड़।

verses.sanskrit:
दातव्यमिति यद्दानं दीयतेऽनुपकारिणे |
देशे काले च पात्रे च तद्दानं सात्त्विकं स्मृतम् ||२०||
verses.translation_en: Charity given out of duty, without expectation of return, at the proper time and place, and to a worthy person is considered to be in the mode of goodness.
verses.translation_hi: 'देना चाहिए' — ऐसी भावना से, उपकार की अपेक्षा न रखकर, उचित देश, काल और पात्र में दिया दान सात्त्विक कहा जाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Datavyam iti yad danam diyate'nupakarine' — charity given with the inner conviction 'it must be given' (datavyam iti — the sense of sacred obligation), to one who has not done any prior service (anupakarine — one who cannot reciprocate, from whom no return is expected or possible). 'Deshe kale ca patre ca' — at the proper place (deshe — a sacred location, a place of learning or service), at the proper time (kale — when it is genuinely needed), and to the proper recipient (patre — literally 'the vessel,' one who can receive and use the gift rightly). 'Tat danam sattvikam smritam' — that charity is remembered as sattvic. Sattvic charity is not merely generous; it is wise generosity — giving the right thing to the right person at the right place and time, from a sense of sacred duty and without expectation of anything in return.
verses.meaning_hi: 'दातव्यमिति यद्दानम्' — 'देना चाहिए' ऐसी भावना से। 'अनुपकारिणे' — उसे जो कुछ भी लौटा नहीं सकता। 'देशे काले च पात्रे च' — उचित स्थान, उचित समय और उचित पात्र में। सात्त्विक दान केवल उदार नहीं बल्कि बुद्धिमान उदारता है — सही चीज सही व्यक्ति को सही स्थान-काल में, पवित्र कर्तव्य की भावना से, बिना किसी प्रत्याशा के।

verses.sanskrit:
यत्तु प्रत्युपकारार्थं फलमुद्दिश्य वा पुनः |
दीयते च परिक्लिष्टं तद्दानं राजसं स्मृतम् ||२१||
verses.translation_en: But charity performed with the expectation of some return, or with a desire for fruitive results, or in a grudging mood, is said to be charity in the mode of passion.
verses.translation_hi: किंतु जो दान प्रत्युपकार की अपेक्षा रखकर, फल को उद्देश्य मानकर अथवा परिक्लेश से दिया जाता है — वह दान राजसिक कहा जाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Yat tu pratyupakarartham' — but that which is given with the expectation of return (pratyupakara — counter-service, reciprocation). 'Phalam uddishya va punah' — or with a specific result in mind. 'Diyate ca pariklishtam' — or given with reluctance, with inner resistance, with a sense of loss. 'Tad danam rajasam smritam' — that charity is remembered as rajasic. The rajasic gift is transactional: it is an investment, not an offering. The giver gives with one eye on the return — whether social, material, karmic, or spiritual. 'Pariklishtam' — given grudgingly, painfully — is particularly revealing: when generosity is accompanied by the inner suffering of letting go of what one holds dear, the gift is contaminated by the ego's reluctance.
verses.meaning_hi: 'प्रत्युपकारार्थम्' — प्रतिफल की अपेक्षा से। 'फलमुद्दिश्य' — फल को लक्ष्य मानकर। 'परिक्लिष्टम्' — अनिच्छा से, भीतरी प्रतिरोध के साथ। राजसिक दान लेन-देन है: यह अर्पण नहीं बल्कि निवेश है। 'परिक्लिष्टम्' विशेष रूप से प्रकाशक है: जब उदारता प्रिय वस्तु छोड़ने की आंतरिक पीड़ा के साथ होती है तो उपहार अहंकार की अनिच्छा से दूषित होता है।

verses.sanskrit:
अदेशकाले यद्दानमपात्रेभ्यश्च दीयते |
असत्कृतमवज्ञातं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ||२२||
verses.translation_en: And charity performed at an improper place and time and given to unworthy persons, without respect and with contempt, is considered to be in the mode of ignorance.
verses.translation_hi: अदेश-काल में, अपात्रों को, असत्कारपूर्वक और अवज्ञापूर्वक दिया गया दान तामसिक कहा जाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Adesha-kale yad danam' — charity given at an improper place and time (adesha — the wrong location; akale — the wrong moment). 'Apatrebhyas ca diyate' — given to unworthy recipients (apatra — the wrong vessel, those who cannot receive the gift constructively). 'Asatkritam' — given without respect, dishonorably. 'Avajnatam' — given with contempt, with the internal attitude of looking down on the recipient. 'Tat tamasam udahritam' — that is called tamasic charity. Tamasic charity fails on every dimension: wrong circumstances, wrong recipient, wrong manner. But the inner quality that defines it most sharply is avajnata — contempt. When charity is given with contempt for the recipient — as a gesture of superiority rather than of genuine care — it degrades both giver and receiver.
verses.meaning_hi: 'अदेशकाले यद्दानम्' — अनुचित देश-काल में। 'अपात्रेभ्यः' — अयोग्य पात्रों को। 'असत्कृतम्' — अनादरपूर्वक। 'अवज्ञातम्' — तिरस्कारपूर्वक। तामसिक दान हर आयाम पर विफल रहता है। लेकिन इसे सबसे तीव्रता से परिभाषित करने वाला आंतरिक गुण 'अवज्ञात' है — तिरस्कार। जब दान पाने वाले के प्रति तिरस्कार के साथ दिया जाता है तो यह दाता और प्राप्तकर्ता दोनों को अपमानित करता है।

verses.sanskrit:
ॐ तत्सदिति निर्देशो ब्रह्मणस्त्रिविधः स्मृतः |
ब्राह्मणास्तेन वेदाश्च यज्ञाश्च विहिताः पुरा ||२३||
verses.translation_en: From the beginning of creation, the three words om tat sat were used to indicate the Supreme Absolute Truth. These three symbolic representations were used by brahmanas while chanting the Vedic hymns and during sacrifices for the satisfaction of the Supreme.
verses.translation_hi: ॐ, तत्, सत् — यह ब्रह्म का त्रिविध निर्देश स्मृत है। इनसे ही पुराने काल में ब्राह्मण, वेद और यज्ञ विहित किए गए।
verses.meaning_en: The chapter now moves to its most mystical dimension: the three sacred syllables that designate the Absolute — OM, TAT, SAT. 'Om tat sat iti nirdesho brahmanah tri-vidhah smritah' — OM, TAT, SAT is the threefold designation of Brahman, the Absolute, as remembered in sacred tradition. These three are not mere words but primary vibrations of reality: OM is the primordial sound, the sound of pure being itself, the syllable from which all manifestation arises. TAT means 'That' — the indefinite pronoun pointing to the Absolute that transcends all names and forms. SAT means 'Being' or 'Truth' — the affirmation of existence itself as the nature of the Real. 'Brahmanah tena vedash ca yajnas ca vihitah pura' — by these, the brahman-teachers, the Vedas, and the sacrifices were established in ancient times.
verses.meaning_hi: अध्याय अब अपने सबसे रहस्यमय आयाम की ओर बढ़ता है: परम को नामित करने वाले तीन पवित्र शब्द — ॐ, तत्, सत्। ये केवल शब्द नहीं बल्कि वास्तविकता के प्राथमिक कंपन हैं: ॐ मूल ध्वनि है, शुद्ध अस्तित्व की ध्वनि। तत् का अर्थ है 'वह' — परम की ओर संकेत करने वाला अनिश्चयवाचक सर्वनाम। सत् का अर्थ है 'सत्ता' या 'सत्य' — वास्तविकता की प्रकृति के रूप में अस्तित्व का स्वीकरण।

verses.sanskrit:
तस्मादोमित्युदाहृत्य यज्ञदानतपःक्रियाः |
प्रवर्तन्ते विधानोक्ताः सततं ब्रह्मवादिनाम् ||२४||
verses.translation_en: Therefore, transcendentalists undertaking performances of sacrifice, charity, and penance in accordance with scriptural regulations always begin with om to attain the Supreme.
verses.translation_hi: इसलिए शास्त्रविधिनुसार यज्ञ, दान और तप की क्रियाएँ ब्रह्मवादियों द्वारा सदा 'ॐ' के उच्चारण से प्रारंभ होती हैं।
verses.meaning_en: 'Tasmad om ity udahritya' — therefore, uttering OM. 'Yajna-dana-tapah-kriyah' — the acts of sacrifice, charity, and austerity. 'Pravartante vidhanokta' — are begun as prescribed. 'Satatam brahma-vadinam' — always by those who speak of Brahman, those for whom the Absolute is the constant reference point of all action. The invocation of OM at the beginning of all sacred acts is not a formality but a metaphysical alignment: it places the act within the context of the Absolute, consecrating it from its very first breath. Every sacrifice, every charitable act, every austerity — when begun with OM — is rooted in the recognition of the Absolute as the ground from which all action arises and to which all action returns.
verses.meaning_hi: 'तस्मादोमित्युदाहृत्य' — इसलिए ॐ का उच्चारण करके। 'यज्ञदानतपःक्रियाः' — यज्ञ, दान और तप की क्रियाएँ। 'ब्रह्मवादिनाम्' — ब्रह्मवादियों द्वारा। ॐ का आह्वान एक औपचारिकता नहीं बल्कि आध्यात्मिक संरेखण है: यह कार्य को परम के संदर्भ में रखता है, उसे उसकी पहली साँस से ही पवित्र करता है।

verses.sanskrit:
तदित्यनभिसन्धाय फलं यज्ञतपःक्रियाः |
दानक्रियाश्च विविधाः क्रियन्ते मोक्षकाङ्क्षिभिः ||२५||
verses.translation_en: Without desiring fruitive results, one should perform various kinds of sacrifice, penance and charity with the word tat. The purpose of such transcendental activities is to get free from material entanglement.
verses.translation_hi: 'तत्' — फल की अभिसंधि न रखकर — यज्ञ, तप और दान की विविध क्रियाएँ मोक्षकांक्षियों द्वारा की जाती हैं।
verses.meaning_en: 'Tad ity anabhisandhaya phalam' — using the word TAT and without any attachment to fruits. 'Yajna-tapah-kriyah dana-kriyash ca vividhah' — the various acts of sacrifice, austerity, and charity. 'Kriyante moksha-kankshi-bhih' — are performed by those who desire liberation (moksha). TAT — 'That' — is the word of renunciation of results. When an act is offered to 'That' — the unnamed, formless Absolute — it is released from the circuit of personal karma. The act is done, the fruit is surrendered to the Absolute, and the doer is freed from the entanglement that would otherwise bind. TAT is the linguistic gesture of surrender, the act of placing one's deeds at the feet of the unnameable.
verses.meaning_hi: 'तदित्यनभिसन्धाय फलम्' — 'तत्' का उपयोग करते हुए, फल की आसक्ति के बिना। 'मोक्षकांक्षिभिः' — मुक्ति की आकांक्षियों द्वारा। 'तत्' — वह — परिणामों के त्याग का शब्द है। जब कोई कार्य 'उस' को — अनाम, निराकार परम को — अर्पित किया जाता है तो यह व्यक्तिगत कर्म के चक्र से मुक्त हो जाता है। 'तत्' समर्पण का भाषाई इशारा है।

verses.sanskrit:
सद्भावे साधुभावे च सदित्येतत्प्रयुज्यते |
प्रशस्ते कर्मणि तथा सच्छब्दः पार्थ युज्यते ||२६||
verses.translation_en: The word sat is used to indicate the nature of the Supreme, and it is also used to indicate a person of transcendental activities. All such activities of sacrifice, austerity, and charity which are in accordance with the Absolute are also indicated by the word sat.
verses.translation_hi: हे पार्थ! सद्भाव और साधुभाव में 'सत्' यह शब्द प्रयुक्त होता है। और उत्तम कर्म में भी 'सत्' शब्द का प्रयोग होता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Sad-bhave sadhu-bhave ca sad ity etat prayujyate' — the word SAT is used in the sense of sat-bhava (being in reality, genuine existence, the quality of being real) and sadhu-bhava (the quality of being good, virtuous, the orientation of the good person). 'Prashaste karmani tatha sac-chbdah Partha yujyate' — and in the sense of excellent, praiseworthy action, O Partha, the word SAT is also used. SAT is thus a word of three registers: ontological (the nature of Being itself), ethical (the quality of the genuinely good person), and practical (the quality of actions that are truly praiseworthy). When any act of sacrifice, austerity, or charity is genuinely aligned with the Real — is authentic, true, and good — it participates in SAT.
verses.meaning_hi: 'सद्भावे साधुभावे च सदित्येतत्प्रयुज्यते' — 'सत्' शब्द सद्भाव और साधुभाव में प्रयुक्त होता है। 'प्रशस्ते कर्मणि तथा सच्छब्दः पार्थ युज्यते' — और उत्तम कर्म में भी। 'सत्' तीन स्तरों का शब्द है: आंटोलॉजिकल (सत्ता की प्रकृति), नैतिक (वास्तव में अच्छे व्यक्ति की गुणवत्ता), और व्यावहारिक (सच्चे प्रशंसनीय कार्यों की गुणवत्ता)।

verses.sanskrit:
यज्ञे तपसि दाने च स्थितिः सदिति चोच्यते |
कर्म चैव तदर्थीयं सदित्येवाभिधीयते ||२७||
verses.translation_en: Steadfastness in sacrifice, austerity, and charity is called sat. And any such activity performed for the sake of the Supreme is also called sat.
verses.translation_hi: यज्ञ, तप और दान में स्थिति 'सत्' कही जाती है — और उनके लिए किया गया कर्म भी 'सत्' ही कहा जाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Yajne tapasi dane ca sthitih sad iti cocyate' — steadfastness (sthiti — being established, remaining) in sacrifice, austerity, and charity — this constancy is called SAT. 'Karma caiva tad-arthiyam sad ity evabhidhiyate' — and any action performed for the sake of That (tad-arthi — for the purpose of the Absolute) is also called SAT. This verse reveals the highest use of the word SAT: it names not just the ontological ground of being but the quality of action that is in alignment with that ground. To remain steadfastly established in the practices of sacrifice, austerity, and charity — to be constant, unwavering, present in one's spiritual life — is itself SAT. And every act, however small, that is oriented toward the Absolute partakes of SAT.
verses.meaning_hi: 'यज्ञे तपसि दाने च स्थितिः सदिति चोच्यते' — यज्ञ, तप और दान में स्थिरता 'सत्' कही जाती है। 'तदर्थीयं सदित्येवाभिधीयते' — और उनके लिए किया कर्म भी। यह श्लोक 'सत्' के उच्चतम उपयोग को उजागर करता है: यह केवल सत्ता के आंटोलॉजिकल आधार को नहीं बल्कि उस आधार के साथ संरेखित कार्य की गुणवत्ता को भी नामित करता है।

verses.sanskrit:
अश्रद्धया हुतं दत्तं तपस्तप्तं कृतं च यत् |
असदित्युच्यते पार्थ न च तत्प्रेत्य नो इह ||२८||
verses.translation_en: But sacrifices, austerities, and charities performed without faith in the Supreme are impermanent, O son of Pritha, regardless of whatever rites are performed. They are called asat and are useless both in this life and in the next.
verses.translation_hi: हे पार्थ! अश्रद्धा से किया गया हवन, दिया गया दान, तपाया गया तप और किया गया कोई भी कर्म — 'असत्' कहलाता है। वह न इहलोक में काम आता है न परलोक में।
verses.meaning_en: 'Ashraddhaya hutam dattam tapas taptam kritam ca yat' — sacrifice offered, charity given, austerity undergone, and any action performed without shraddha (faith). 'Asad ity ucyate Partha' — it is called ASAT, O Partha. 'Na ca tat pretya no iha' — and it benefits neither after death nor here in this life. The closing verse of Chapter 17 brings the entire teaching to its single root. All the elaborate taxonomies of the chapter — three kinds of food, three kinds of sacrifice, three kinds of austerity (body, speech, mind), three kinds of charity, the three sacred syllables OM TAT SAT — all of it ultimately reduces to one question: is there shraddha? Is there genuine, authentic faith? Without it, every outer form — however correct, however elaborate, however sincere in appearance — is ASAT, non-being, ineffectual. With it, even the simplest act participates in the Real.
verses.meaning_hi: 'अश्रद्धया हुतं दत्तं तपस्तप्तं कृतं च यत्' — श्रद्धा के बिना किया यज्ञ, दान, तप और कोई भी कर्म। 'असदित्युच्यते पार्थ' — 'असत्' कहलाता है। 'न च तत्प्रेत्य नो इह' — न परलोक में काम आता है न इहलोक में। अध्याय 17 का समापन श्लोक पूरी शिक्षा को उसकी एकमात्र जड़ पर लाता है: क्या श्रद्धा है? क्या वास्तविक, प्रामाणिक आस्था है? इसके बिना, हर बाह्य रूप — चाहे कितना भी सही, विस्तृत या ईमानदार दिखे — 'असत्' है, निरर्थक। इसके साथ, सबसे सरल कार्य भी वास्तविक में भागीदार होता है।

chapters.subtitle_en: The Yoga of Liberation and Renunciation
chapters.summary_en: The final and longest chapter brings together all the teachings. Krishna concludes with the essence of all religion: surrender to God with love. He assures Arjuna of liberation through this surrender.
chapters.summary_hi: अंतिम और सबसे लंबा अध्याय सभी शिक्षाओं को एक साथ लाता है। कृष्ण सभी धर्म के सार के साथ समाप्त करते हैं: प्रेम के साथ ईश्वर को समर्पण।

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच |
सन्न्यासस्य महाबाहो तत्त्वमिच्छामि वेदितुम् |
त्यागस्य च हृषीकेश पृथक्केशिनिषूदन ||१||
verses.translation_en: Arjuna said: O mighty-armed one, I wish to understand the purpose of renunciation and of the renounced order of life, O killer of the Keshi demon, master of the senses.
verses.translation_hi: अर्जुन बोले: हे महाबाहो! हे हृषीकेश! हे केशिनिषूदन! मैं संन्यास और त्याग के तत्त्व को पृथक् रूप से जानना चाहता हूँ।
verses.meaning_en: The final chapter of the Gita opens with Arjuna asking for a clarification that the entire preceding seventeen chapters have been building toward: the distinction between sannyas (renunciation of action itself, the complete abandonment of the world) and tyaga (renunciation of the fruits of action while continuing to act). These are not synonyms but two different spiritual postures, and confusion between them has been a source of misunderstanding throughout the dialogue. Arjuna addresses Krishna by three names: Maha-bahu (mighty-armed — the strength to act), Hrishikesha (master of the senses — the one whose senses are fully under control), and Keshinisudana (slayer of the Keshi demon — the one who destroys the forces of delusion). Each name points to a quality relevant to the question: genuine renunciation requires strength, sense-mastery, and the destruction of the inner demonic forces of ego.
verses.meaning_hi: गीता का अंतिम अध्याय उस प्रश्न से खुलता है जिसके लिए पिछले सत्रह अध्याय निर्माण करते रहे हैं: संन्यास (कर्म का त्याग, संसार का पूर्ण परित्याग) और त्याग (कर्म के फलों का त्याग, कर्म जारी रखते हुए) के बीच का भेद। अर्जुन तीन नामों से पुकारता है: महाबाहो (कार्य करने की शक्ति), हृषीकेश (इन्द्रियों के स्वामी), केशिनिषूदन (भ्रम की आसुरी शक्तियों का नाशक)। प्रत्येक नाम प्रश्न से प्रासंगिक गुण की ओर संकेत करता है।

verses.sanskrit:
श्रीभगवानुवाच |
काम्यानां कर्मणां न्यासं सन्न्यासं कवयो विदुः |
सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं प्राहुस्त्यागं विचक्षणाः ||२||
verses.translation_en: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: The giving up of activities that are based on material desire is what great learned men call the renounced order of life. And giving up the results of all activities is what the wise call renunciation.
verses.translation_hi: श्रीभगवान बोले: काम्य कर्मों के त्याग को विद्वान संन्यास कहते हैं; और सभी कर्मों के फलों के त्याग को विचक्षण त्याग कहते हैं।
verses.meaning_en: 'Kamyanam karmanam nyasam sannyasam kavayo viduh' — the abandonment (nyasa) of kama-driven actions (kamya karma — actions performed with desire for specific results) is what the learned (kavayo — the wise seers) call sannyas. And: 'sarva-karma-phala-tyagam prahus tyagam vicakshanah' — the relinquishment of the fruits of all actions is what the discerning (vicakshanah) call tyaga. The distinction crystallizes: sannyas is about the category of action (abandoning desire-motivated actions), while tyaga is about the relationship to consequences (relinquishing results of all actions, including obligatory ones). The Gita's position, as will unfold through this chapter, is that tyaga — not sannyas in the sense of world-abandonment — is the more complete and universally applicable path.
verses.meaning_hi: 'काम्यानां कर्मणां न्यासं संन्यासं कवयो विदुः' — काम्य कर्मों का परित्याग विद्वान संन्यास कहते हैं। 'सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं प्राहुस्त्यागं विचक्षणाः' — सभी कर्मों के फलों का त्याग विचक्षण त्याग कहते हैं। भेद स्पष्ट होता है: संन्यास कर्म की श्रेणी के बारे में है, जबकि त्याग परिणामों के साथ संबंध के बारे में। गीता की स्थिति यह होगी कि त्याग अधिक पूर्ण और सार्वभौमिक रूप से लागू मार्ग है।

verses.sanskrit:
त्याज्यं दोषवदित्येके कर्म प्राहुर्मनीषिणः |
यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यमिति चापरे ||३||
verses.translation_en: Some learned men declare that all kinds of fruitive activities should be given up as faulty, yet other sages maintain that acts of sacrifice, charity, and penance should never be abandoned.
verses.translation_hi: कुछ मनीषी कहते हैं कि कर्म दोषयुक्त है अतः त्याज्य है; और दूसरे कहते हैं कि यज्ञ, दान और तप के कर्म त्याज्य नहीं।
verses.meaning_en: Krishna acknowledges the genuine philosophical debate that existed in his time — and that continues to exist. One school holds that all action is contaminated by desire and its consequences, and therefore all action should be abandoned (tyajyam doshavat). The other school holds that the three fundamental acts of sacred life — yajna (sacrifice), dana (charity), tapas (austerity) — are never to be abandoned (na tyajyam) because they are the very fabric of dharmic existence. This is not a trivial disagreement: it goes to the heart of how one understands the relationship between action and liberation. Krishna will now resolve it with his characteristic precision.
verses.meaning_hi: कृष्ण उस वास्तविक दार्शनिक बहस को स्वीकार करते हैं जो उनके समय में थी। एक विचारधारा: सभी कर्म इच्छा से दूषित हैं, इसलिए सभी का त्याग करो। दूसरी विचारधारा: यज्ञ, दान और तप के तीन मूलभूत पवित्र कार्य कभी त्याज्य नहीं। यह मामूली असहमति नहीं: यह कर्म और मुक्ति के बीच संबंध के मूल तक जाती है। कृष्ण अब इसे अपनी विशिष्ट सटीकता से हल करेंगे।

verses.sanskrit:
निश्चयं शृणु मे तत्र त्यागे भरतसत्तम |
त्यागो हि पुरुषव्याघ्र त्रिविधः सम्प्रकीर्तितः ||४||
verses.translation_en: O best of the Bharatas, now hear My judgment about renunciation. O tiger among men, renunciation is declared in the scriptures to be of three kinds.
verses.translation_hi: हे भरतसत्तम! हे पुरुषव्याघ्र! त्याग के विषय में मेरा निश्चय सुनो। त्याग तीन प्रकार का कहा गया है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Nishcayam shrinu me tatra tyage Bharata-sattama' — hear My definitive judgment (nishcayam — settled conclusion, certainty) on the matter of tyaga. The two epithets here are significant: 'Bharata-sattama' (best of the Bharatas — the finest of your lineage) and 'purusha-vyaghra' (tiger among men — one of outstanding strength and nobility). Krishna addresses Arjuna at the height of his potential, as the being he is capable of being, not as the confused warrior of Chapter 1. The teaching that follows is given to the best, the noblest, the strongest — because it demands all of those qualities to receive and embody it. Tyaga is threefold, as everything in the Gita's world is threefold.
verses.meaning_hi: 'निश्चयं शृणु मे तत्र त्यागे भरतसत्तम' — त्याग के विषय में मेरा निश्चित निष्कर्ष सुनो। दो उपाधियाँ यहाँ महत्वपूर्ण हैं: 'भरतसत्तम' और 'पुरुषव्याघ्र'। कृष्ण अर्जुन को उसकी क्षमता की ऊँचाई पर संबोधित करते हैं, उस श्रेष्ठ प्राणी के रूप में जो वह बन सकता है। त्याग त्रिविध है।

verses.sanskrit:
यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यं कार्यमेव तत् |
यज्ञो दानं तपश्चैव पावनानि मनीषिणाम् ||५||
verses.translation_en: Acts of sacrifice, charity, and penance are not to be given up; they must be performed. Indeed, sacrifice, charity, and penance purify even the great souls.
verses.translation_hi: यज्ञ, दान और तप के कर्म त्याज्य नहीं — वे तो करने ही चाहिए। यज्ञ, दान और तप मनीषियों को भी पवित्र करते हैं।
verses.meaning_en: 'Yajna-dana-tapah-karma na tyajyam karyam eva tat' — acts of yajna, dana, and tapas are not to be abandoned; indeed, they must be performed (karyam eva — it is precisely what must be done). The verb is emphatic: not merely permissible but obligatory. 'Yajno danam tapash caiva pavananani manishinam' — sacrifice, charity, and austerity purify (pavana — they cleanse, they sanctify) even the wise (manishinam — the thoughtful, the careful souls). Even those who have realized their inner purity still perform these acts — not because they need purification but because these acts are the sustaining fabric of the world (as taught in Chapter 3) and because their performance, without attachment to results, deepens and expresses the realization they already have.
verses.meaning_hi: 'यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यं कार्यमेव तत्' — यज्ञ, दान और तप के कर्म त्याज्य नहीं — वे तो करने ही हैं। क्रिया जोरदार है: केवल अनुमन्य नहीं बल्कि अनिवार्य। 'पावनानि मनीषिणाम्' — ये मनीषियों को भी पवित्र करते हैं। जिन्होंने आंतरिक शुद्धता का अनुभव किया है वे भी इन कार्यों को करते हैं — क्योंकि ये जगत के सहारे का ताना-बाना हैं।

verses.sanskrit:
एतान्यपि तु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलानि च |
कर्तव्यानीति मे पार्थ निश्चितं मतमुत्तमम् ||६||
verses.translation_en: All these activities should be performed without attachment or any expectation of result. They should be performed as a matter of duty, O son of Pritha. That is My final opinion.
verses.translation_hi: हे पार्थ! ये सभी कर्म संग और फल को त्यागकर कर्तव्य समझकर करने चाहिए — यह मेरा निश्चित और उत्तम मत है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Etany api tu karmani sangam tyaktva phalani ca' — but even these acts (yajna, dana, tapas) should be performed having abandoned attachment (sanga) and fruits (phalani). 'Kartavyaniti me Partha nishcitam matam uttamam' — as duty, as what must be done — this is My definitive (nishcitam) and highest (uttamam) view. The resolution of the philosophical debate is elegant: the second school is correct that yajna, dana, and tapas must never be abandoned. But the first school is also pointing to something real: the attachment to results that contaminates action must be abandoned. The answer is not the abandonment of the acts but the abandonment of the self-interested relationship to their fruits. Act fully; surrender the fruits completely.
verses.meaning_hi: 'एतान्यपि तु कर्माणि संगं त्यक्त्वा फलानि च' — ये कर्म भी संग और फल को त्यागकर। 'कर्तव्यानीति मे पार्थ निश्चितं मतमुत्तमम्' — कर्तव्य समझकर — यह मेरा निश्चित और उत्तम मत है। दार्शनिक बहस का समाधान सुंदर है: कर्मों का त्याग नहीं, बल्कि फलों के साथ स्वार्थी संबंध का त्याग। पूर्णतः कर्म करो; फलों को पूरी तरह समर्पित करो।

verses.sanskrit:
नियतस्य तु सन्न्यासः कर्मणो नोपपद्यते |
मोहात्तस्य परित्यागस्तामसः परिकीर्तितः ||७||
verses.translation_en: Prescribed duties should never be renounced. If one gives up prescribed duties because of illusion, such renunciation is said to be in the mode of ignorance.
verses.translation_hi: नियत कर्म का संन्यास उचित नहीं। मोह से उसका परित्याग तामसिक कहा जाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Niyatasya tu sannyasah karmano nopapadyate' — the renunciation of niyata karma (prescribed duties, those actions one is obligated to perform by one's station, nature, and circumstances) is simply not appropriate. 'Mohat tasya parityagas tamasah parikirtitah' — its abandonment out of delusion (moha — the fundamental confusion about what is real and what one's responsibilities are) is declared to be tamasic. This is the first of three forms of tyaga, defined by the guna it expresses. Tamasic renunciation abandons duty not from transcendence but from confusion, laziness, or the unwillingness to face the difficulties that genuine duty entails. It mistakes avoidance for renunciation and calls its own spiritual inertia by the noble name of detachment.
verses.meaning_hi: 'नियतस्य तु संन्यासः कर्मणो नोपपद्यते' — नियत कर्म का संन्यास उचित नहीं। 'मोहात्तस्य परित्यागस्तामसः परिकीर्तितः' — मोह से इसका परित्याग तामसिक कहा जाता है। तामसिक त्याग कर्तव्य को अतिक्रमण से नहीं बल्कि भ्रम, आलस्य, या वास्तविक कर्तव्य की कठिनाइयों का सामना करने की अनिच्छा से छोड़ता है। यह परिहार को त्याग समझता है।

verses.sanskrit:
दुःखमित्येव यत्कर्म कायक्लेशभयात्त्यजेत् |
स कृत्वा राजसं त्यागं नैव त्यागफलं लभेत् ||८||
verses.translation_en: Anyone who gives up prescribed duties as troublesome or out of fear of bodily discomfort is said to have renounced in the mode of passion. Such action never leads to the elevation of renunciation.
verses.translation_hi: जो कर्म को कष्टप्रद समझकर और शारीरिक क्लेश के भय से त्यागता है — वह राजसिक त्याग करता है और त्याग का फल नहीं पाता।
verses.meaning_en: 'Duhkham ity eva yat karma kaya-klesha-bhayat tyajet' — whatever action is abandoned saying 'this is painful,' out of fear of physical hardship (kaya-klesha-bhaya). 'Sa kritva rajasam tyagam naiva tyaga-phalam labhet' — having performed such rajasic renunciation, one does not at all obtain the fruit of renunciation. Rajasic renunciation is motivated not by moha (the confusion of tamas) but by the rajasic desire to avoid discomfort, to escape difficulty, to flee from what is hard. It is the renunciation of convenience: 'I give this up because it is too difficult, too painful, too demanding.' This is not detachment but avoidance — dressed in spiritual language to avoid the embarrassment of admitting that one simply did not want to do the hard thing.
verses.meaning_hi: 'दुःखमित्येव यत्कर्म कायक्लेशभयात्त्यजेत्' — कर्म को कष्टप्रद कहकर, शारीरिक क्लेश के भय से त्यागना। 'राजसं त्यागं' — राजसिक त्याग — इच्छाशक्ति की कमी नहीं बल्कि असुविधा से बचने की राजसिक इच्छा से प्रेरित। यह सुविधा का त्याग है: 'मैं यह इसलिए छोड़ता हूँ क्योंकि यह बहुत कठिन है।' यह वैराग्य नहीं बल्कि परिहार है।

verses.sanskrit:
कार्यमित्येव यत्कर्म नियतं क्रियतेऽर्जुन |
सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलं चैव स त्यागः सात्त्विको मतः ||९||
verses.translation_en: O Arjuna, when one performs his prescribed duty only because it ought to be done, and renounces all material association and all attachment to the fruit, his renunciation is said to be in the mode of goodness.
verses.translation_hi: हे अर्जुन! जो नियत कर्म 'करना चाहिए' — यही समझकर संग और फल को त्यागकर किया जाता है — वह त्याग सात्त्विक माना जाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Karyam ity eva yat karma niyatam kriyate Arjuna' — whatever prescribed action is done simply because it ought to be done (karyam iti — with the inner conviction of obligation, of rightness). 'Sangam tyaktva phalam caiva' — having abandoned attachment (sanga) and fruit (phala). 'Sa tyagah sattviko matah' — that renunciation is considered sattvic. The sattvic tyagi neither avoids duty out of confusion (tamas) nor abandons it out of discomfort (rajas), nor performs it with attachment to results (which would not be tyaga at all). The sattvic tyagi performs what must be performed — fully, completely, with all available skill and energy — and then releases the outcome entirely. This is the core teaching: not 'do nothing' but 'do everything without the clutch of personal investment in the result.'
verses.meaning_hi: 'कार्यमित्येव यत्कर्म नियतं क्रियतेऽर्जुन' — जो नियत कर्म 'करना चाहिए' यह समझकर किया जाए। 'संगं त्यक्त्वा फलं चैव स त्यागः सात्त्विको मतः' — संग और फल त्यागकर — वह सात्त्विक त्याग। सात्त्विक त्यागी न तमस से कर्तव्य से बचता है, न रजस से असुविधा से भागता है। वह जो करना चाहिए उसे पूर्णतः करता है — और फिर परिणाम पूरी तरह छोड़ देता है।

verses.sanskrit:
न द्वेष्ट्यकुशलं कर्म कुशले नानुषज्जते |
त्यागी सत्त्वसमाविष्टो मेधावी छिन्नसंशयः ||१०||
verses.translation_en: The intelligent renouncer situated in the mode of goodness, who neither hates inauspicious work nor is attached to auspicious work, has no doubts about work.
verses.translation_hi: जो अकुशल कर्म से द्वेष नहीं करता और कुशल कर्म में आसक्त नहीं — ऐसा सत्त्वसमाविष्ट, मेधावी और छिन्नसंशय त्यागी है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Na dveshty akushalam karma kushale nanushajjate' — does not hate inauspicious (difficult, unpleasant) work and is not attached to auspicious (pleasant, rewarding) work. The sattvic tyagi is equally available to both pleasant and unpleasant duties: he does not recoil from what is hard, nor does he cling to what is agreeable. 'Tyagi sattva-samavishto medhavi chhinna-samshayah' — the renouncer who is filled with sattva (sattva-samavishto), who is medhavi (one of discriminative intelligence — one who can distinguish the essential from the inessential), and who is chhinna-samshayah (one whose doubts have been cut — who has moved from the uncertainty of the seeker to the clarity of the knower). These three qualities together — equanimity, discriminative wisdom, and freedom from doubt — constitute the realized tyagi.
verses.meaning_hi: 'न द्वेष्ट्यकुशलं कर्म कुशले नानुषज्जते' — अप्रिय कर्म से द्वेष नहीं, प्रिय कर्म में आसक्ति नहीं। सात्त्विक त्यागी दोनों के प्रति समान रूप से उपलब्ध है। 'सत्त्वसमाविष्टो मेधावी छिन्नसंशयः' — सत्त्व से भरा, विवेकशील, और संशयरहित। ये तीन गुण — समभाव, विवेकपूर्ण ज्ञान और संशयमुक्ति — साथ मिलकर साकार त्यागी का निर्माण करते हैं।

verses.sanskrit:
न हि देहभृता शक्यं त्यक्तुं कर्माण्यशेषतः |
यस्तु कर्मफलत्यागी स त्यागीत्यभिधीयते ||११||
verses.translation_en: It is indeed impossible for an embodied being to give up all activities. But one who renounces the fruits of action is called one who has truly renounced.
verses.translation_hi: देहधारी के लिए कर्मों को पूरी तरह त्यागना संभव नहीं। किंतु जो कर्मफलत्यागी है — वही त्यागी कहलाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Na hi deha-bhrita shakyam tyaktum karmany asheshatah' — it is truly not possible for an embodied being to abandon actions entirely and completely. The body itself is action: breathing is action, digestion is action, the beating of the heart is action. To be embodied is to be in action. 'Yas tu karma-phala-tyagi sa tyagi ty abhidhiyate' — but the one who renounces the fruits of action — that person is called the renouncer. This verse resolves the apparent paradox of the entire Gita: the path is not actionlessness (which is impossible for the embodied) but action-without-attachment-to-fruits. The genuine tyagi is not the one who has found a way to do nothing but the one who does everything while holding onto nothing.
verses.meaning_hi: 'न हि देहभृता शक्यं त्यक्तुं कर्माण्यशेषतः' — देहधारी के लिए कर्मों को पूरी तरह त्यागना वास्तव में संभव नहीं। शरीर स्वयं कर्म है। 'यस्तु कर्मफलत्यागी स त्यागीत्यभिधीयते' — किंतु कर्मफलत्यागी — वही त्यागी। यह श्लोक पूरी गीता के स्पष्ट विरोधाभास को हल करता है: मार्ग अकर्म नहीं बल्कि फलासक्ति के बिना कर्म है। सच्चा त्यागी वह है जो सब कुछ करते हुए किसी चीज को थामता नहीं।

verses.sanskrit:
अनिष्टमिष्टं मिश्रं च त्रिविधं कर्मणः फलम् |
भवत्यत्यागिनां प्रेत्य न तु सन्न्यासिनां क्वचित् ||१२||
verses.translation_en: For one who is not renounced, the threefold fruits of action — desirable, undesirable, and mixed — accrue after death. But those who are in the renounced order of life have no such result to suffer or enjoy.
verses.translation_hi: अत्यागियों को मृत्यु के बाद तीन प्रकार के कर्मफल — अनिष्ट, इष्ट और मिश्र — मिलते हैं। किंतु संन्यासियों को कहीं भी नहीं।
verses.meaning_en: 'Anishtam ishtam mishram ca tri-vidham karmanah phalam' — the threefold fruit of action: undesirable (anishta), desirable (ishta), and mixed (mishra). 'Bhavaty atyaginam pretya' — these accrue after death to those who have not renounced (atyaginam — those who have not practiced tyaga). 'Na tu sannyasinam kvacit' — but never at all to those who are truly sannyasins (those who have genuinely practiced tyaga). The principle is the law of karma: action produces fruits, and those fruits must be experienced — either in this life, or after death in the configurations of the next. But the genuine renouncer — the one who acts without attachment to results — does not accumulate this karmic fruit. The action is performed; the fruit is released; there is nothing to be harvested.
verses.meaning_hi: 'त्रिविधं कर्मणः फलम्' — कर्म का त्रिविध फल: अनिष्ट, इष्ट और मिश्र। 'भवत्यत्यागिनां प्रेत्य' — ये मृत्यु के बाद अत्यागियों को प्राप्त होते हैं। 'न तु संन्यासिनां क्वचित्' — सच्चे संन्यासियों को कहीं भी नहीं। सच्चा त्यागी — जो फलासक्ति के बिना कर्म करता है — कर्मिक फल संचित नहीं करता। कर्म होता है; फल छोड़ा जाता है; संचय के लिए कुछ शेष नहीं।

verses.sanskrit:
पञ्चैतानि महाबाहो कारणानि निबोध मे |
साङ्ख्ये कृतान्ते प्रोक्तानि सिद्धये सर्वकर्मणाम् ||१३||
verses.translation_en: O mighty-armed Arjuna, according to the Vedanta philosophy there are five causes for the accomplishment of every action. Now learn of these from Me.
verses.translation_hi: हे महाबाहो! सांख्य-कृतान्त में सभी कर्मों की सिद्धि के लिए पाँच कारण कहे गए हैं — उन्हें मुझसे समझो।
verses.meaning_en: 'Pancaitani maha-baho karanani nibodha me' — O mighty-armed one, understand from Me these five causes. 'Sankhye kritante proktani siddhaye sarva-karmanam' — declared in the Sankhya-kritanta (the final, conclusive analysis of Sankhya philosophy) for the accomplishment of all actions. This verse opens a remarkable philosophical passage: a systematic analysis of the metaphysics of action, intended to free Arjuna (and us) from the most fundamental misunderstanding about agency — the belief that 'I' alone am the doer of my actions. The five causes to be enumerated are the complete explanation of why this belief is a distortion and what the actual structure of action really is.
verses.meaning_hi: 'पञ्चैतानि महाबाहो कारणानि निबोध मे' — हे महाबाहो! इन पाँच कारणों को समझो। 'साङ्ख्ये कृतान्ते प्रोक्तानि सिद्धये सर्वकर्मणाम्' — सांख्य-कृतान्त में सभी कर्मों की सिद्धि के लिए। यह श्लोक एक उल्लेखनीय दार्शनिक अंश खोलता है: कर्म की तत्त्वमीमांसा का व्यवस्थित विश्लेषण — एजेंसी के बारे में सबसे मूलभूत गलतफहमी से मुक्त करने के लिए।

verses.sanskrit:
अधिष्ठानं तथा कर्ता करणं च पृथग्विधम् |
विविधाश्च पृथक्चेष्टा दैवं चैवात्र पञ्चमम् ||१४||
verses.translation_en: The place of action [the body], the performer, the various senses, the many different kinds of endeavor, and ultimately the Supersoul — these are the five factors of action.
verses.translation_hi: अधिष्ठान (शरीर), कर्ता, विविध करण, विभिन्न चेष्टाएँ और पाँचवाँ दैव — ये पाँच कारण हैं।
verses.meaning_en: The five causes of all action are enumerated: 1. 'Adhisthanam' — the seat or basis of action (the body, the field, the locus in which action occurs). 2. 'Karta' — the doer (the ego-sense, the sense of personal agency that believes it is acting). 3. 'Karanam ca prithag-vidham' — the various instruments (the senses — cognitive and active — through which action flows). 4. 'Vividhash ca prithak ceshtah' — the various separate efforts and movements (the vital forces, the pranas, the specific energies that animate particular actions). 5. 'Daivam caiva atra pancamam' — and daiva as the fifth here (the unseen dimension, the providential factor, the cosmic/divine order that presides over all). No single one of these five is the action; action is the result of all five in concert.
verses.meaning_hi: कर्म के पाँच कारण: 1. 'अधिष्ठान' — कर्म का आधार (शरीर)। 2. 'कर्ता' — कर्ता (अहंकार-भाव)। 3. 'करणं पृथग्विधम्' — विभिन्न उपकरण (इन्द्रियाँ)। 4. 'विविधाश्च पृथक्चेष्टाः' — विभिन्न चेष्टाएँ (प्राण-शक्तियाँ)। 5. 'दैवं पञ्चमम्' — दैव पाँचवाँ (अदृश्य, विधाता, ब्रह्मांडीय व्यवस्था)। इनमें से कोई एक अकेला कर्म नहीं है; कर्म इन पाँचों के समवेत का परिणाम है।

verses.sanskrit:
शरीरवाङ्मनोभिर्यत्कर्म प्रारभते नरः |
न्याय्यं वा विपरीतं वा पञ्चैते तस्य हेतवः ||१५||
verses.translation_en: Whatever right or wrong action a man performs by body, mind, or speech is caused by these five factors.
verses.translation_hi: मनुष्य शरीर, वाणी और मन से जो भी न्याय्य या विपरीत कर्म प्रारंभ करता है — उसके ये पाँचों ही हेतु हैं।
verses.meaning_en: 'Sharira-van-manobhir yat karma prarabhate narah' — whatever action a person initiates through body (sharira), speech (vak), or mind (manas). 'Nyayyam va viparitam va' — whether right (nyayyam — proper, just) or contrary (viparitam — wrong, improper). 'Pancaite tasya hetavah' — these five are its causes. The universality of the five-cause analysis is here declared: every action, of every kind, at every level (physical, verbal, mental), whether morally right or morally wrong — all of it flows from the same five causes. This is an enormously liberating ontology: no single isolated 'I' is the author of any action; every action is the intersection of the body, the ego-sense, the instruments, the vital energies, and the cosmic order. Understanding this frees the Self from its false claim to exclusive agency.
verses.meaning_hi: 'शरीरवाङ्मनोभिर्यत्कर्म प्रारभते नरः' — शरीर, वाणी और मन से जो भी कर्म। 'न्याय्यं वा विपरीतं वा' — न्याय्य हो या विपरीत। 'पञ्चैते तस्य हेतवः' — ये पाँच ही इसके हेतु हैं। पाँच-कारण विश्लेषण की सार्वभौमिकता: हर कर्म, हर प्रकार का, हर स्तर पर — सभी इन्हीं पाँच कारणों के मिलन से होते हैं। यह विशाल मुक्तिदायी दर्शन है।

verses.sanskrit:
तत्रैवं सति कर्तारमात्मानं केवलं तु यः |
पश्यत्यकृतबुद्धित्वान्न स पश्यति दुर्मतिः ||१६||
verses.translation_en: Therefore, one who thinks himself the only doer, not considering the five factors, is certainly not very intelligent and cannot see things as they are.
verses.translation_hi: फिर भी जो अकृतबुद्धि के कारण केवल आत्मा को ही कर्ता देखता है — वह दुर्मति सही नहीं देखता।
verses.meaning_en: 'Tatraivam sati kartaram atmanam kevalam tu yah' — yet one who, in this situation (where action has five causes), sees the self alone (kevalam — only, exclusively) as the doer. 'Pashyaty akrita-buddhitvan' — perceives thus because of immature/undeveloped intelligence (akrita-buddhi — intelligence that has not been formed, refined). 'Na sa pashyati durmatih' — that person of wrong understanding (dur-mati — bad mind, perverted thinking) does not see. The one who says 'I did this' — as if the body, the senses, the vital energies, and the cosmic order had no part in it — is suffering from a fundamental perceptual error. It is this error — the error of isolated, exclusive agency — that is the root of both pride (when outcomes are good) and guilt (when outcomes are bad), and ultimately of all karma-bondage.
verses.meaning_hi: 'कर्तारमात्मानं केवलं तु यः पश्यति' — जो आत्मा को ही एकमात्र कर्ता देखता है। 'अकृतबुद्धित्वात्' — अपरिपक्व बुद्धि के कारण। 'न स पश्यति दुर्मतिः' — वह दुर्मति सही नहीं देखता। जो कहता है 'यह मैंने किया' — मानो शरीर, इन्द्रियाँ और ब्रह्मांडीय व्यवस्था का इसमें कोई भाग नहीं — वह एक मूलभूत अवधारणात्मक भूल से ग्रस्त है। यह भूल ही अहंकार और अपराधबोध दोनों की जड़ है।

verses.sanskrit:
यस्य नाहङ्कृतो भावो बुद्धिर्यस्य न लिप्यते |
हत्वापि स इमाँल्लोकान्न हन्ति न निबध्यते ||१७||
verses.translation_en: One who is not motivated by false ego, whose intelligence is not entangled, though he kills men in this world, does not kill. Nor is he bound by his actions.
verses.translation_hi: जिसका भाव अहंकृत नहीं और जिसकी बुद्धि लिप्त नहीं — वह इन लोकों को मारकर भी न मारता है, न बँधता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Yasya nahankrito bhavo buddhir yasya na lipyate' — one whose inner state (bhava) is not ego-made (nahankritah — not produced by the false ego) and whose intelligence is not entangled (na lipyate — not smeared, not contaminated). 'Hatvaapi sa iman lokan na hanti na nibadhyate' — even having killed people in this world, he neither kills nor is bound. This is one of the most radical statements in the Gita — and the most relevant for Arjuna on his actual battlefield. The one who acts without ego-authorship, whose inner instrument remains unclouded by the claim of personal doership, is not bound by the action's results, however dramatic those results appear to the outer eye. The action passes through him like wind through space — fully present, fully engaged, and utterly untainted.
verses.meaning_hi: 'यस्य नाहंकृतो भावो बुद्धिर्यस्य न लिप्यते' — जिसका भाव अहंकारकृत नहीं और बुद्धि लिप्त नहीं। 'हत्वापि स इमाँल्लोकान्न हन्ति न निबध्यते' — इन लोकों को मारकर भी न मारता है, न बँधता है। यह गीता के सबसे कट्टरपंथी और अर्जुन के लिए सबसे प्रासंगिक कथनों में से एक है। जो अहंकार-स्वामित्व के बिना कार्य करता है, वह क्रिया के परिणामों से बंधा नहीं।

verses.sanskrit:
ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं परिज्ञाता त्रिविधा कर्मचोदना |
करणं कर्म कर्तेति त्रिविधः कर्मसङ्ग्रहः ||१८||
verses.translation_en: Knowledge, the object of knowledge, and the knower are the three factors that motivate action; the senses, the work, and the doer compose the threefold basis of action.
verses.translation_hi: ज्ञान, ज्ञेय और परिज्ञाता — ये तीन कर्म-प्रेरणाएँ हैं; करण, कर्म और कर्ता — यह तीन प्रकार का कर्म-संग्रह है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Jnanam jneyam parijnaata tri-vidha karma-codana' — knowledge (jnanam), the object to be known (jneyam), and the knower (parijnaata) — these three are the triple impulse, the triple spur that drives action. Before any action occurs, there must be a knower, something to be known, and the act of knowing itself. 'Karanam karma karteti tri-vidhah karma-sangrahah' — the instrument (karanam — the senses), the act (karma — the action itself), and the doer (karta — the agent) — this is the triple basis, the triple composition of action. Two triads are presented: the epistemological triad (knower-known-knowing) and the practical triad (instrument-act-agent). Together they provide the complete formal structure of how any action comes to be.
verses.meaning_hi: 'ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं परिज्ञाता त्रिविधा कर्मचोदना' — ज्ञान, ज्ञेय और परिज्ञाता — ये तीन कर्म-प्रेरणाएँ हैं। कोई भी कर्म होने से पहले एक ज्ञाता, कुछ जानने योग्य, और ज्ञान का कार्य होना चाहिए। 'करणं कर्म कर्तेति त्रिविधः कर्मसंग्रहः' — उपकरण, कर्म और कर्ता — त्रिविध कर्म-संग्रह। दो त्रिक: ज्ञान-मीमांसात्मक (ज्ञाता-ज्ञेय-ज्ञान) और व्यावहारिक (उपकरण-कर्म-कर्ता)।

verses.sanskrit:
ज्ञानं कर्म च कर्ता च त्रिधैव गुणभेदतः |
प्रोच्यते गुणसङ्ख्याने यथावच्छृणु तान्यपि ||१९||
verses.translation_en: According to the three different modes of material nature, there are three kinds of knowledge, action, and performer of action. Now hear of them as declared in the science of the modes.
verses.translation_hi: गुणसंख्यान में ज्ञान, कर्म और कर्ता भी गुणभेद से तीन-तीन प्रकार के कहे जाते हैं — उन्हें यथाविधि सुनो।
verses.meaning_en: 'Jnanam karma ca karta ca tridhativa guna-bhedata' — knowledge, action, and the doer — each is threefold according to the distinction of the gunas (guna-bhedata — differentiated by which guna predominates). 'Procyate guna-sankhyane yathavat shrinu tany api' — they are declared in the Sankhya analysis of the gunas; hear them as they are. The next nine verses (20-28) will systematically analyze knowledge (verses 20-22), action (verses 23-25), and the doer (verses 26-28) through the lens of the three gunas. This is one of the Gita's most comprehensive and penetrating analyses: by applying the guna-framework to the very structure of knowing, doing, and being-a-doer, Krishna dissolves the false monolith of 'action' into its constituent qualities, making visible the inner structure of every human deed.
verses.meaning_hi: 'ज्ञानं कर्म च कर्ता च त्रिधैव गुणभेदतः' — ज्ञान, कर्म और कर्ता — प्रत्येक गुणभेद से तीन प्रकार का है। अगले नौ श्लोक (20-28) व्यवस्थित रूप से ज्ञान (20-22), कर्म (23-25) और कर्ता (26-28) का तीन गुणों के दृष्टिकोण से विश्लेषण करेंगे। यह गीता के सबसे व्यापक विश्लेषणों में से एक है।

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वभूतेषु येनैकं भावमव्ययमीक्षते |
अविभक्तं विभक्तेषु तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि सात्त्विकम् ||२०||
verses.translation_en: That knowledge by which one undivided spiritual nature is seen in all living entities, though they are divided into innumerable forms, you should understand to be in the mode of goodness.
verses.translation_hi: जिस ज्ञान से सभी प्राणियों में एक अव्यय भाव को, विभक्त होने पर भी अविभक्त को देखा जाता है — उसे सात्त्विक जानो।
verses.meaning_en: 'Sarva-bhuteshu yenaikam bhavam avyayam ikshate' — that by which one sees in all living beings a single, imperishable (avyayam) reality (bhavam). 'Avibhaktam vibhakteshu' — undivided (avibhaktam) within what appears divided (vibhakteshu) — the one within the many, the unity within the apparent multiplicity of forms. 'Taj jnanam viddhi sattvikam' — know that knowledge to be sattvic. Sattvic knowledge is the knowledge of unity: the vision that beneath all the apparent diversity of forms — human and animal, male and female, plant and stone — there is one imperishable reality, one consciousness, one Being. This is not theoretical monism but a direct perceptual transformation: actually seeing the one in the many, the way sunlight is seen as one even when it plays on ten thousand different surfaces.
verses.meaning_hi: 'सर्वभूतेषु येनैकं भावमव्ययमीक्षते' — जिससे सभी प्राणियों में एक अव्यय भाव देखा जाए। 'अविभक्तं विभक्तेषु' — विभक्त में अविभक्त — अनेक में एक, प्रकट बहुलता के भीतर एकता। 'तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि सात्त्विकम्' — उसे सात्त्विक ज्ञान जानो। सात्त्विक ज्ञान एकता का ज्ञान है: वह दर्शन जो सभी प्रकट विविधताओं के नीचे एक अव्यय सत्ता देखता है।

verses.sanskrit:
पृथक्त्वेन तु यज्ज्ञानं नानाभावान्पृथग्विधान् |
वेत्ति सर्वेषु भूतेषु तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि राजसम् ||२१||
verses.translation_en: That knowledge by which one sees that in every different body there is a different type of living entity you should understand to be in the mode of passion.
verses.translation_hi: किंतु जो ज्ञान पृथकत्व के कारण सभी प्राणियों में नाना भावों को पृथग्विध रूप से जानता है — उसे राजसिक जानो।
verses.meaning_en: 'Prithaktvena tu yaj jnanam nana-bhavan prithag-vidhan vetti sarveshu bhuteshu' — but the knowledge by which one knows, through the lens of separateness (prithaktvena), different and various (nana — many, varied; prithag-vidhan — of distinct kinds) natures in all beings. 'Taj jnanam viddhi rajasam' — know that knowledge to be rajasic. Rajasic knowledge is the knowledge of multiplicity without unity: it sees all the differences (of species, gender, class, nationality, personality) accurately enough but does not penetrate to the unifying ground beneath the differences. It is the knowledge that divides, categorizes, and differentiates — enormously useful for practical navigation of the world but insufficient for liberation, because it cannot see the one Self in all and therefore cannot dissolve the boundary between self and other.
verses.meaning_hi: 'पृथक्त्वेन तु यज्ज्ञानं नानाभावान्पृथग्विधान्' — पृथकत्व के दृष्टिकोण से सभी प्राणियों में नाना भेद जानना। 'तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि राजसम्' — उसे राजसिक जानो। राजसिक ज्ञान एकता के बिना बहुलता का ज्ञान है: यह सभी अंतरों को सटीक रूप से देखता है लेकिन उनके नीचे की एकीकृत भूमि तक नहीं पहुँचता।

verses.sanskrit:
यत्तु कृत्स्नवदेकस्मिन्कार्ये सक्तमहैतुकम् |
अतत्त्वार्थवदल्पं च तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ||२२||
verses.translation_en: And that knowledge by which one is attached to one kind of work as the all in all, without knowledge of the truth, and which is very meager, is said to be in the mode of darkness.
verses.translation_hi: जो ज्ञान एक ही कार्य में सम्पूर्ण की तरह आसक्त, हेतुरहित, अतत्त्वार्थवाला और अल्प है — वह तामसिक कहा जाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Yat tu kritsnavad ekasmin karye saktam ahaitukam' — but that which is attached (saktam) to a single object or pursuit as if it were the whole (kritsnavat — as the entirety), without reason or understanding (ahaitukam — groundless, without proper basis). 'Atattvartha-vad alpam ca' — not in accordance with reality (atattvartha — without the meaning of truth), and insignificant, narrow (alpam). 'Tat tamasam udahritam' — that is called tamasic knowledge. Tamasic knowledge is the knowledge that mistakes a part for the whole: the single-track mind that is fixated on one small domain of experience and inflates it to cosmic proportions. It lacks both the sattvic vision of underlying unity and the rajasic awareness of genuine multiplicity. It is narrow, obsessive, groundless, and out of touch with reality.
verses.meaning_hi: 'कृत्स्नवदेकस्मिन्कार्ये सक्तम्' — एक ही विषय में सम्पूर्ण की तरह आसक्त। 'अहैतुकम्' — आधारहीन। 'अतत्त्वार्थवदल्पम्' — सत्य के अर्थ के बिना, संकीर्ण। 'तामसमुदाहृतम्' — तामसिक। तामसिक ज्ञान अंश को पूर्ण समझता है: एकल-मार्गी मन जो एक छोटे क्षेत्र पर स्थिर है और उसे ब्रह्मांडीय अनुपात में फुला देता है।

verses.sanskrit:
नियतं सङ्गरहितमरागद्वेषतः कृतम् |
अफलप्रेप्सुना कर्म यत्तत्सात्त्विकमुच्यते ||२३||
verses.translation_en: That action which is regulated and which is performed without attachment, without love or hatred, and without desire for fruitive results is said to be in the mode of goodness.
