verses.translation_hi: जो कर्म नियत है, संगरहित है, राग-द्वेष से रहित है और फल की इच्छा से रहित व्यक्ति द्वारा किया गया है — वह सात्त्विक कहलाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Niyatam sanga-rahitam araga-dveshatah kritam' — action that is regulated (niyatam — prescribed, ordered), free from attachment (sanga-rahitam), performed without passion (araga — without desire-coloring) and without aversion (dveshata — without repulsion). 'Aphala-prepsuna karma yat tat sattvikam ucyate' — action performed by one who is not seeking fruit (aphala-prepsuna — one who does not hanker after results), that is called sattvic. The qualities of sattvic action map precisely onto the qualities of the sattvic tyagi from verse 9: regulated, dispassionate, free from both attraction and aversion, and completely indifferent to personal result. This is action as pure service — action in which the ego has become transparent, a channel for duty rather than a vehicle for self-advancement.
verses.meaning_hi: 'नियतं संगरहितमरागद्वेषतः कृतम्' — नियत, संगरहित, राग-द्वेष से रहित। 'अफलप्रेप्सुना कर्म यत्तत्सात्त्विकमुच्यते' — फल की इच्छा न करने वाले द्वारा — वह सात्त्विक। सात्त्विक कर्म के गुण शुद्ध सेवा के रूप में कर्म की ओर संकेत करते हैं — जिसमें अहंकार पारदर्शी हो जाता है।

verses.sanskrit:
यत्तु कामेप्सुना कर्म साहङ्कारेण वा पुनः |
क्रियते बहुलायासं तद्राजसमुदाहृतम् ||२४||
verses.translation_en: But action performed with great effort by one who seeks to gratify his desires, and enacted from a sense of false ego, is called action in the mode of passion.
verses.translation_hi: किंतु जो कर्म इच्छुक द्वारा, अहंकारसहित, बहुत परिश्रम से किया जाता है — वह राजसिक कहा जाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Yat tu kamepsuna karma sahankareṇa va punah' — but action performed by one who desires (kamepsuna — hungry for, seeking) results, and with ego (sahankareṇa — accompanied by the sense of personal doership, 'I am doing this'). 'Kriyate bahulayasam' — done with great effort and strain (bahulayasam — involving much labor, much expenditure of energy, much grinding exertion). 'Tad rajasam udahritam' — that is called rajasic. The rajasic action is effort-heavy: it is the action of the person who is straining, pushing, forcing outcomes, working with the tension of someone whose self-worth is invested in the result. The great effort is not evidence of dedication but of the anxiety of the ego that needs the outcome to turn out a particular way.
verses.meaning_hi: 'कामेप्सुना कर्म साहंकारेण' — इच्छुक द्वारा, अहंकारसहित। 'बहुलायासम्' — बहुत परिश्रम से। 'तद्राजसमुदाहृतम्' — राजसिक। राजसिक कर्म श्रम-भारी है: उस व्यक्ति का कर्म जो फल में अपना आत्म-मूल्य लगाए है। बहुत प्रयास समर्पण का प्रमाण नहीं बल्कि उस अहंकार की चिंता है।

verses.sanskrit:
अनुबन्धं क्षयं हिंसामनपेक्ष्य च पौरुषम् |
मोहादारभ्यते कर्म यत्तत्तामसमुच्यते ||२५||
verses.translation_en: That action performed in illusion, in disregard of scriptural injunctions, and without concern for future bondage or for violence or distress caused to others is said to be in the mode of ignorance.
verses.translation_hi: जो कर्म भविष्य के बंधन, क्षति, हिंसा और अपनी शक्ति की उपेक्षा करके मोह से प्रारंभ किया जाता है — वह तामसिक कहलाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Anubandham kshayam himsam anapekshya ca pausham' — without regard for future bondage (anubandham — the binding consequences that will follow), without regard for destruction (kshayam — the loss, the damage), without regard for harm to others (himsam — violence, injury), without regard for one's own capacity (paurusham — one's actual ability and strength). 'Mohadaarabhyate karma yat tat tamasam ucyate' — action begun out of delusion (mohat), that is called tamasic. Tamasic action is blind action: blind to consequences, blind to the harm it causes, blind to its own limits, launched from the fog of tamas without thought, without discrimination, without care. It is not merely mistaken but actively dangerous, because it combines the momentum of action with the absence of any discernment about where that momentum is headed.
verses.meaning_hi: 'अनुबन्धं क्षयं हिंसामनपेक्ष्य' — भविष्य के बंधन, क्षति और हिंसा की अनदेखी। 'मोहादारभ्यते कर्म यत्तत्तामसमुच्यते' — मोह से आरंभ किया कर्म तामसिक। तामसिक कर्म अंधा कर्म है: परिणामों के प्रति अंधा, कारण होने वाली हानि के प्रति, अपनी सीमाओं के प्रति। यह खतरनाक है क्योंकि यह कर्म की गति को विवेक के अभाव के साथ जोड़ता है।

verses.sanskrit:
मुक्तसङ्गोऽनहंवादी धृत्युत्साहसमन्वितः |
सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योर्निर्विकारः कर्ता सात्त्विक उच्यते ||२६||
verses.translation_en: One who performs his duty without association with the modes of material nature, without false ego, with great determination and enthusiasm, and without wavering in success or failure is said to be a worker in the mode of goodness.
verses.translation_hi: जो मुक्तसंग, अनहंवादी, धृति और उत्साह से युक्त, सिद्धि-असिद्धि में निर्विकार है — वह कर्ता सात्त्विक कहलाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Mukta-sango'naham-vadi dhrity-utsaha-samanvitah' — free from attachment (mukta-sangah), without the claim of 'I am the doer' (anahm-vadi — not a proclaimer of 'I'), endowed with dhrti (steadiness, firmness of purpose — the quality that persists through difficulty) and utsaha (enthusiasm — the quality of fresh, alive engagement with the work). 'Siddhy-asiddhyor nirvikarah' — unchanging (nirvikarah — without modification, without disturbance) in both success (siddhi) and failure (asiddhi). 'Karta sattvika ucyate' — such a doer is called sattvic. The sattvic doer is not passive or mechanical but vibrantly engaged: full of steadiness and enthusiasm, fully committed to the work, and yet utterly unperturbed by whether the work succeeds or fails. This is the portrait of the ideal karma-yogi.
verses.meaning_hi: 'मुक्तसंगः अनहंवादी धृत्युत्साहसमन्वितः' — आसक्ति से मुक्त, 'मैं कर्ता' का दावा न करने वाला, धृति और उत्साह से युक्त। 'सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योर्निर्विकारः' — सफलता और असफलता दोनों में अविचल। 'कर्ता सात्त्विक उच्यते' — वह सात्त्विक कर्ता। यह आदर्श कर्मयोगी का चित्र है: पूरी तरह प्रतिबद्ध, फिर भी परिणाम से पूरी तरह अप्रभावित।

verses.sanskrit:
रागी कर्मफलप्रेप्सुर्लुब्धो हिंसात्मकोऽशुचिः |
हर्षशोकान्वितः कर्ता राजसः परिकीर्तितः ||२७||
verses.translation_en: The worker who is attached to his work and the fruits of his work, who desires to enjoy those results, who is greedy, always envious, impure, and moved by joy and sorrow, is said to be in the mode of passion.
verses.translation_hi: जो कर्मफल की इच्छा रखने वाला, लोभी, हिंसात्मक स्वभाव वाला, अशुचि और हर्ष-शोक से युक्त है — वह राजसिक कर्ता कहलाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Ragi karma-phala-prepsur lubdho himsatmako'shuci' — attached (ragi — full of raga, desire-filled), seeking the fruits of action (karma-phala-prepsu — hungering for results), greedy (lubdha), violent in nature (hims-atmaka — whose nature tends toward harm), impure (ashuchi — inner uncleanliness of motivation). 'Harsha-shokarvitah karta rajasah parikirtitah' — the doer who is accompanied by elation (harsha) and grief (shoka) — such a doer is proclaimed to be rajasic. The rajasic doer rides the emotional roller-coaster of the attached ego: elated when things go their way, devastated when they don't. This emotional volatility — the harsha-shoka oscillation — is the most reliable sign of rajasic action: the degree of emotional disturbance in success and failure reveals the degree of ego-investment in the outcome.
verses.meaning_hi: 'रागी कर्मफलप्रेप्सुः लुब्धो हिंसात्मकोऽशुचिः' — आसक्त, फलेच्छु, लोभी, हिंसात्मक, अशुद्ध। 'हर्षशोकान्वितः कर्ता राजसः परिकीर्तितः' — हर्ष-शोक से युक्त कर्ता राजसिक। राजसिक कर्ता आसक्त अहंकार की भावनात्मक उथल-पुथल पर सवार है। हर्ष-शोक का उतार-चढ़ाव राजसिक कर्म का सबसे विश्वसनीय संकेत है।

verses.sanskrit:
अयुक्तः प्राकृतः स्तब्धः शठो नैष्कृतिकोऽलसः |
विषादी दीर्घसूत्री च कर्ता तामस उच्यते ||२८||
verses.translation_en: The worker who is always engaged in work against the injunctions of the scripture, who is materialistic, obstinate, cheating, expert in insulting others, lazy, morose, and procrastinating is said to be a worker in the mode of ignorance.
verses.translation_hi: जो अयुक्त, प्राकृत, स्तब्ध, शठ, नैष्कृतिक, आलसी, विषादी और दीर्घसूत्री है — वह तामसिक कर्ता कहलाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Ayuktah' — not connected (to shastra, to proper guidance, to the wisdom that would orient action rightly). 'Prakritah' — vulgar, materialistic (concerned only with the gross, the obvious, the immediate). 'Stabdhah' — rigid, stubborn (the inflexibility of tamas). 'Shathah' — dishonest, deceitful. 'Naishkritikah' — given to insulting others, to putting others down. 'Alasah' — lazy, inert. 'Vishadi' — morose, despondent, dispirited. 'Dirgha-sutri' — procrastinating (dirgha-sutra — one who draws things out, who never quite begins or completes). 'Karta tamasa ucyate' — such a doer is called tamasic. Eight qualities paint the portrait of the tamasic doer: disconnected, gross, rigid, dishonest, contemptuous, lazy, despondent, and procrastinating. Tamas is the principle of inertia in human character — the weight that resists transformation.
verses.meaning_hi: 'अयुक्तः' — शास्त्र से असंबद्ध। 'प्राकृतः' — अशिष्ट, भौतिकवादी। 'स्तब्धः' — कठोर। 'शठः' — बेईमान। 'नैष्कृतिकः' — दूसरों का अपमान करने वाला। 'आलसः' — आलसी। 'विषादी' — उदास। 'दीर्घसूत्री' — ढिलाई करने वाला। 'कर्ता तामस उच्यते' — तामसिक कर्ता। आठ गुण तामसिक कर्ता का चित्र बनाते हैं। तमस मानव चरित्र में जड़ता का सिद्धांत है।

verses.sanskrit:
बुद्धेर्भेदं धृतेश्चैव गुणतस्त्रिविधं शृणु |
प्रोच्यमानमशेषेण पृथक्त्वेन धनञ्जय ||२९||
verses.translation_en: O winner of wealth, now please listen as I tell you in detail of the different kinds of understanding and determination, according to the three modes of material nature.
verses.translation_hi: हे धनञ्जय! गुणों के अनुसार बुद्धि और धृति के तीन-तीन भेद अशेष रूप से पृथक् कहे जाते हैं — उन्हें सुनो।
verses.meaning_en: The analysis of the three gunas as they manifest in the different dimensions of the person continues: having covered knowledge (jnana), action (karma), and the doer (karta), Krishna now turns to buddhi (intelligence/understanding) and dhrti (steadiness/determination). 'Buddher bhedam dhritesh caiva gunatas tri-vidham shrinu' — hear the threefold distinction (bheda) of buddhi and dhrti according to the gunas. 'Procyamanam asheshena prithaktvena Dhananjaya' — declared completely (asheshena — without remainder) and separately (prithaktvena). The address 'Dhananjaya' (winner of wealth) is significant: the wealth being won here is not material but the incomparably valuable wealth of discriminative wisdom about one's own inner faculties.
verses.meaning_hi: तीन गुणों का विभिन्न आयामों में विश्लेषण जारी: ज्ञान, कर्म और कर्ता के बाद अब बुद्धि और धृति। 'बुद्धेर्भेदं धृतेश्चैव गुणतस्त्रिविधं शृणु' — गुणों के अनुसार बुद्धि और धृति के त्रिविध भेद सुनो। 'धनञ्जय' संबोधन महत्वपूर्ण: यहाँ जीती जाने वाली संपत्ति भौतिक नहीं बल्कि अपनी आंतरिक क्षमताओं के बारे में विवेकपूर्ण ज्ञान की अमूल्य संपत्ति है।

verses.sanskrit:
प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च कार्याकार्ये भयाभये |
बन्धं मोक्षं च या वेत्ति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी ||३०||
verses.translation_en: O son of Pritha, that understanding by which one knows what ought to be done and what ought not to be done, what is to be feared and what is not to be feared, what is binding and what is liberating, is in the mode of goodness.
verses.translation_hi: हे पार्थ! जो बुद्धि प्रवृत्ति और निवृत्ति, कार्य और अकार्य, भय और अभय, बंधन और मोक्ष को जानती है — वह सात्त्विकी बुद्धि है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Pravrittim ca nivrittim ca karyakaryam bhayabhaye' — the sattvic buddhi knows: pravritti (what to move toward, what action to engage in) and nivritti (what to turn away from, what to abstain from); karya (what must be done, duty) and akarya (what must not be done, the prohibited); bhaya (what is genuinely to be feared — the spiritual dangers that lead to bondage) and abhaya (what is not to be feared). 'Bandham moksham ca ya vetti buddhih sa Partha sattviki' — and which knows bondage (bandam — the conditions of limitation) and liberation (moksham). The sattvic intelligence is the intelligence of complete spiritual orientation: it knows the full map — not just what to do and not do, but what leads toward freedom and what leads toward bondage. It is the intelligence that can read the territory of one's own inner life.
verses.meaning_hi: 'प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च कार्याकार्ये भयाभये' — सात्त्विक बुद्धि जानती है: क्या करना है और क्या नहीं, कर्तव्य और वर्जना, वास्तविक भय और अभय। 'बन्धं मोक्षं च या वेत्ति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी' — और जो बंधन और मोक्ष को जानती है। सात्त्विक बुद्धि पूर्ण आध्यात्मिक अभिमुखता की बुद्धि है: यह पूरा नक्शा जानती है।

verses.sanskrit:
यया धर्ममधर्मं च कार्यं चाकार्यमेव च |
अयथावत्प्रजानाति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ राजसी ||३१||
verses.translation_en: O son of Pritha, that understanding which cannot distinguish between religion and irreligion, between action that should be done and action that should not be done, is in the mode of passion.
verses.translation_hi: हे पार्थ! जो बुद्धि धर्म और अधर्म को तथा कार्य और अकार्य को यथावत् नहीं जानती — वह राजसी बुद्धि है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Yaya dharmam adharmam ca karyam cakaryam eva ca' — that which concerns dharma and adharma (the right and the wrong, the ordered and the disordered) and karya and akarya (what to do and what not to do). 'Ayathavat prajanati buddhih sa Partha rajasi' — understands (prajanati) these inaccurately (ayathavat — not as they truly are), O Partha — that buddhi is rajasic. Rajasic intelligence has enough intelligence to know that there are distinctions between right and wrong, between what should and should not be done — but it gets them wrong. Its discrimination is real but distorted, warped by the rajasic drives of desire, ego, and the need to justify what one wants to do. The rajasic mind can argue convincingly for both sides of any ethical question, and it will tend to argue for whichever side serves its desires.
verses.meaning_hi: 'यया धर्ममधर्मं च कार्यं चाकार्यमेव च' — धर्म-अधर्म और कार्य-अकार्य। 'अयथावत्प्रजानाति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ राजसी' — इन्हें यथावत् नहीं जानती — वह राजसी बुद्धि। राजसी बुद्धि में सही और गलत के बीच अंतर जानने की पर्याप्त बुद्धि है — किंतु वह गलत होती है। उसका विवेक वास्तविक लेकिन विकृत है।

verses.sanskrit:
अधर्मं धर्ममिति या मन्यते तमसावृता |
सर्वार्थान्विपरीतांश्च बुद्धिः सा पार्थ तामसी ||३२||
verses.translation_en: That understanding which considers irreligion to be religion and religion to be irreligion, under the spell of illusion and darkness, and strives always in the wrong direction, O Partha, is in the mode of ignorance.
verses.translation_hi: हे पार्थ! जो बुद्धि तमस से आवृत होकर अधर्म को धर्म और सभी पदार्थों को विपरीत मानती है — वह तामसी बुद्धि है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Adharmam dharmam iti ya manyate tamasavrita' — that which, covered by tamas, believes adharma to be dharma (the wrong to be right, the harmful to be beneficial). 'Sarva-arthan viparitams ca buddhih sa Partha tamasi' — and sees all things in a reversed (viparitam — inverted, upside-down) manner, O Partha — that intelligence is tamasic. Tamasic intelligence is not merely inaccurate (rajasic) but inverted: it has darkness so thoroughly covering its perceptual faculty that it actually sees things the wrong way round. What is dark appears light; what is harmful appears beneficial; what is wrong appears right. This is not a minor perceptual error but a systematic inversion of moral and spiritual reality — the deepest form of delusion, in which the person is utterly unable to distinguish their confusion from clarity.
verses.meaning_hi: 'अधर्मं धर्ममिति या मन्यते तमसावृता' — जो तमस से आवृत होकर अधर्म को धर्म मानती है। 'सर्वार्थान्विपरीतांश्च बुद्धिः सा पार्थ तामसी' — सभी पदार्थों को उल्टा देखती है — वह तामसी। तामसी बुद्धि केवल गलत नहीं बल्कि उल्टी है: इसमें तमस इतना गहरा है कि यह सब कुछ उल्टा देखती है। यह मामूली अवधारणात्मक भूल नहीं बल्कि नैतिक वास्तविकता का व्यवस्थित व्युत्क्रम है।

verses.sanskrit:
धृत्या यया धारयते मनःप्राणेन्द्रियक्रियाः |
योगेनाव्यभिचारिण्या धृतिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी ||३३||
verses.translation_en: O son of Pritha, that determination which is unbreakable, which is sustained with steadfastness by use of yoga practice, and which thus controls the activities of the mind, life, and senses is determination in the mode of goodness.
verses.translation_hi: हे पार्थ! जो धृति अव्यभिचारी योग से मन, प्राण और इन्द्रियों की क्रियाओं को धारण करती है — वह सात्त्विकी धृति है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Dhritya yaya dharayate manah-prana-indriya-kriyah' — the steadiness (dhrti) by which one holds together (dharayate — sustains, maintains) the activities of the mind (manah), the vital force (prana), and the senses (indriya). 'Yogena avyabhicharinyah' — through yoga, without deviation (avyabhichari — not straying, unwavering). 'Dhritih sa Partha sattviki' — that steadiness, O Partha, is sattvic. Sattvic dhrti is the integrating steadiness that holds the whole person together in the service of the spiritual path: mind, breath, and senses all gathered and directed by the practice of yoga. It is not the grim determination of willpower but the organic coherence of a person whose inner life is genuinely unified, whose various faculties are not at war with each other but moving together in one direction.
verses.meaning_hi: 'धृत्या यया धारयते मनःप्राणेन्द्रियक्रियाः' — जो धृति मन, प्राण और इन्द्रियों की क्रियाओं को धारण करती है। 'योगेनाव्यभिचारिण्या' — अव्यभिचारी योग से। 'धृतिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी' — वह सात्त्विकी धृति। सात्त्विक धृति संपूर्ण व्यक्ति को आध्यात्मिक मार्ग की सेवा में एकत्र करती है: मन, श्वास और इन्द्रियाँ सभी एक दिशा में।

verses.sanskrit:
यया तु धर्मकामार्थान्धृत्या धारयतेऽर्जुन |
प्रसङ्गेन फलाकाङ्क्षी धृतिः सा पार्थ राजसी ||३४||
verses.translation_en: But that determination by which one holds fast to fruitive results in religion, economic development, and sense gratification is of the nature of passion, O Arjuna.
verses.translation_hi: हे पार्थ! किंतु जो धृति प्रसंग के कारण फलाकांक्षी होकर धर्म, काम और अर्थ को धारण करती है — वह राजसी धृति है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Yaya tu dharma-kamartha-n dhritya dharayate'rjuna' — but the steadiness by which one holds on to dharma (religious obligation), kama (desire, sensory pleasure), and artha (wealth, material prosperity) — the classical three human aims. 'Prasangena phalakankshi' — with attachment (prasangena — through entanglement, through the clinging of involvement) and desire for results (phala-kankshi — hungering for fruits). 'Dhritih sa Partha Rajasi' — that steadiness, O Partha, is rajasic. The rajasic person is also determined — they hold firmly to their goals — but their goals are the goals of the ego: religious observance for its rewards, pleasure, wealth. The steadiness serves the personal agenda. It is genuine steadiness but misdirected — the considerable energy of determination channeled into the circuit of desire and acquisition.
verses.meaning_hi: 'यया तु धर्मकामार्थान्धृत्या धारयते' — धर्म, काम और अर्थ को धारण करती धृति। 'प्रसंगेन फलाकांक्षी' — आसक्ति और फलाकांक्षा के साथ। 'धृतिः सा पार्थ राजसी' — वह राजसी धृति। राजसिक व्यक्ति भी दृढ़ है — लेकिन उनके लक्ष्य अहंकार के लक्ष्य हैं। दृढ़ता सेवा करती है व्यक्तिगत एजेंडे की।

verses.sanskrit:
यया स्वप्नं भयं शोकं विषादं मदमेव च |
न विमुञ्चति दुर्मेधा धृतिः सा पार्थ तामसी ||३५||
verses.translation_en: And that determination which cannot go beyond dreaming, fearfulness, lamentation, moroseness, and illusion — such unintelligent determination, O son of Pritha, is in the mode of darkness.
verses.translation_hi: हे पार्थ! जो दुर्मेधा धृति स्वप्न, भय, शोक, विषाद और मद को नहीं छोड़ती — वह तामसी धृति है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Yaya svapnam bhayam shokam vishadam madam eva ca' — that by which one does not release (na vimunchati) sleep/dreaming (svapnam — the stupor of unconsciousness, the tamasic inability to be awake and present), fear (bhayam), grief (shokam), despondency (vishadam — the sinking feeling, the loss of spirit), and intoxication (madam — the state of being puffed up, deluded by self-importance). 'Na vimunchati durmedha' — the person of poor intelligence (durmedha) does not release these. 'Dhritih sa Partha tamasi' — that steadiness is tamasic. The tamasic person holds on — stubbornly, fixedly — but what they hold on to is their own darkness: they are determined in their fear, determined in their grief, determined in their delusion. Tamas is the principle that keeps one stuck — not in virtue or truth, but in the states that obscure both.
verses.meaning_hi: 'यया स्वप्नं भयं शोकं विषादं मदमेव च न विमुंचति' — जो नींद/स्वप्न, भय, शोक, विषाद और मद को नहीं छोड़ती। 'दुर्मेधा' — दुर्बुद्धि। 'धृतिः सा पार्थ तामसी' — वह तामसी धृति। तामसिक व्यक्ति थामता है — जिद्दी, अडिग — लेकिन जो थामता है वह अपना ही अंधकार है: वह अपने भय में, अपने शोक में, अपने मोह में दृढ़ है।

verses.sanskrit:
सुखं त्विदानीं त्रिविधं शृणु मे भरतर्षभ |
अभ्यासाद्रमते यत्र दुःखान्तं च निगच्छति ||३६||
verses.translation_en: O best of the Bharatas, now please hear from Me about the three kinds of happiness by which the conditioned soul enjoys, and by which he sometimes comes to the end of all distress.
verses.translation_hi: हे भरतर्षभ! अब मुझसे तीन प्रकार के सुख को सुनो — जिसमें अभ्यास से रमण होता है और जो दुःख के अंत तक ले जाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Sukham tv idanim tri-vidham shrinu me Bharata-rshabha' — now hear from Me, O best of the Bharatas, the three kinds of happiness. The analysis of the three gunas continues with its final domain: sukha (happiness, pleasure, enjoyment). 'Abhyasad ramate yatra duhkhantam ca nigacchati' — happiness in which the conditioned soul delights through practice and by which one comes to the end of all suffering (duhkhantam — the cessation of duhkha). This last phrase — 'duhkhantam' — is crucial: not all three forms of sukha lead to the end of suffering. Only one does. The taxonomy of happiness is also a map of the different kinds of pleasure available to human beings, and an analysis of which of those pleasures is genuinely freeing and which — however attractive — leads deeper into bondage.
verses.meaning_hi: 'सुखं त्विदानीं त्रिविधं शृणु मे भरतर्षभ' — अब तीन प्रकार के सुख को सुनो। 'अभ्यासाद्रमते यत्र दुःखान्तं च निगच्छति' — जिसमें अभ्यास से रमण और जो दुःखान्त तक ले जाता है। यह अंतिम वाक्यांश महत्वपूर्ण: तीनों सुख दुःख के अंत की ओर नहीं ले जाते। केवल एक जाता है।

verses.sanskrit:
यत्तदग्रे विषमिव परिणामेऽमृतोपमम् |
तत्सुखं सात्त्विकं प्रोक्तमात्मबुद्धिप्रसादजम् ||३७||
verses.translation_en: That which in the beginning may be just like poison but at the end is just like nectar and which awakens one to self-realization is said to be happiness in the mode of goodness.
verses.translation_hi: जो आरम्भ में विष के समान और परिणाम में अमृत के समान है — वह आत्मबुद्धिप्रसाद से उत्पन्न सात्त्विक सुख कहलाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Yat tad agre visham iva pariname amritopamam' — that which in the beginning (agre) is like poison (visham iva) and at the end (pariname) is like nectar (amritopamam). 'Tat sukham sattvikam proktam atma-buddhi-prasadajam' — that happiness is called sattvic, born of the clarity of the intelligence focused on the Self (atma-buddhi-prasada — the serene luminosity of the intelligence directed toward the atma). The sattvic happiness is the happiness of spiritual practice: in the beginning it demands effort, it requires the abandonment of easier, more immediately gratifying pleasures, it involves discipline that can feel like hardship. But at its culmination, it yields the taste of the immortal — the profound, inexhaustible joy of the Self known directly.
verses.meaning_hi: 'यत्तदग्रे विषमिव परिणामेऽमृतोपमम्' — जो आरम्भ में विष जैसा और परिणाम में अमृत जैसा। 'आत्मबुद्धिप्रसादजम्' — आत्मा की ओर निर्देशित बुद्धि की प्रसन्नता से उत्पन्न। सात्त्विक सुख आध्यात्मिक अभ्यास का सुख है: आरम्भ में प्रयास माँगता है लेकिन अंत में अमृत का स्वाद देता है।

verses.sanskrit:
विषयेन्द्रियसंयोगाद्यत्तदग्रेऽमृतोपमम् |
परिणामे विषमिव तत्सुखं राजसं स्मृतम् ||३८||
verses.translation_en: That happiness which is derived from contact of the senses with their objects and which appears like nectar at first but poison at the end is said to be of the nature of passion.
verses.translation_hi: विषय और इन्द्रियों के संयोग से उत्पन्न जो सुख आरम्भ में अमृत जैसा और परिणाम में विष जैसा है — वह राजसिक सुख कहलाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Vishayendriya-samyogad yat tad agre amritopamam' — that which comes from the union (samyoga) of the sense objects (vishaya) with the senses (indriya), which at first appears like nectar. 'Pariname visham iva tat sukham rajasam smritam' — at the end like poison — that happiness is remembered as rajasic. Rajasic happiness is the exact reversal of sattvic: it begins as nectar (the rush of sensory pleasure, the satisfaction of desire fulfilled) and ends as poison (the hangover, the emptiness, the craving for more, the disease from overindulgence, the boredom and restlessness that follow every indulgence). This is not a moral judgment but an empirical observation: the pleasures of sensory indulgence genuinely do begin as sweet and end as bitter — this is simply the pattern of addictive and compulsive pleasure.
verses.meaning_hi: 'विषयेन्द्रियसंयोगाद्यत्तदग्रेऽमृतोपमम्' — इन्द्रिय-विषय के संयोग से, आरम्भ में अमृत जैसा। 'परिणामे विषमिव तत्सुखं राजसं स्मृतम्' — परिणाम में विष जैसा — राजसिक। राजसिक सुख सात्त्विक का ठीक उल्टा है: अमृत से शुरू होकर विष पर समाप्त। यह नैतिक निर्णय नहीं बल्कि अनुभवजन्य अवलोकन है।

verses.sanskrit:
यदग्रे चानुबन्धे च सुखं मोहनमात्मनः |
निद्रालस्यप्रमादोत्थं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ||३९||
verses.translation_en: And that happiness which is blind to self-realization, which is delusion from beginning to end and which arises from sleep, laziness, and illusion is said to be of the nature of ignorance.
verses.translation_hi: जो सुख आरम्भ में और अनुबंध में भी आत्मा को मोहित करने वाला है, निद्रा, आलस्य और प्रमाद से उत्पन्न है — वह तामसिक कहा जाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Yad agre ca anubandhe ca sukham mohanam atmanah' — that happiness which is delusive to the Self both at the beginning (agre) and in continuation (anubandhe). 'Nidra-lasya-pramadottha' — arising from sleep (nidra), laziness (lasya), and heedlessness (pramada). 'Tat tamasam udahritam' — that is called tamasic. Tamasic happiness has no redemptive arc: it is not bitter only at the end (like rajasic) but delusive throughout — at the beginning, during, and in its aftermath. It deludes the Self — it obscures consciousness, it reduces awareness, it confirms and deepens the tamas that gives rise to it. The happiness of excessive sleep, of indolent avoidance of life's demands, of heedless intoxication — these leave the person progressively more confused, more inert, more cut off from genuine vitality.
verses.meaning_hi: 'यदग्रे चानुबन्धे च सुखं मोहनमात्मनः' — जो आरम्भ में और अनुबंध में भी आत्मा को मोहित करता है। 'निद्रालस्यप्रमादोत्थम्' — निद्रा, आलस्य और प्रमाद से उत्पन्न। 'तामसमुदाहृतम्' — तामसिक। तामसिक सुख में कोई मुक्तिदायी चाप नहीं: यह आरम्भ से अंत तक भ्रामक है।

verses.sanskrit:
न तदस्ति पृथिव्यां वा दिवि देवेषु वा पुनः |
सत्त्वं प्रकृतिजैर्मुक्तं यदेभिः स्यात्त्रिभिर्गुणैः ||४०||
verses.translation_en: There is no being existing, either here or among the demigods in the higher planetary systems, which is freed from these three modes of material nature.
verses.translation_hi: पृथ्वी पर या स्वर्ग में देवताओं में भी ऐसा कोई प्राणी नहीं जो प्रकृति से उत्पन्न इन तीन गुणों से मुक्त हो।
verses.meaning_en: 'Na tad asti prithivyam va divi deveshu va punah' — there is no such being in the earth or even among the gods in heaven. 'Sattvam prakrti-jair muktam yad ebhih syat tribhir gunaih' — no being (sattvam) born of prakriti that would be free from these three gunas. The universality of the teaching is here declared absolutely: not on earth, not in heaven, not among the most elevated divine beings — no one born within the field of prakriti is free from the three gunas. The gunas are the very fabric of manifestation; to be embodied at all is to be in the play of the gunas. This is not a statement of despair but a clarification: liberation is not the absence of the gunas (which is impossible for the embodied) but the transcendence of identification with them.
verses.meaning_hi: 'न तदस्ति पृथिव्यां वा दिवि देवेषु वा' — पृथ्वी पर या देवताओं में भी ऐसा नहीं। 'सत्त्वं प्रकृतिजैर्मुक्तं यदेभिः स्यात्त्रिभिर्गुणैः' — प्रकृतिजन्म जो इन तीन गुणों से मुक्त हो। शिक्षा की सार्वभौमिकता पूर्णतः घोषित: कोई भी प्रकट प्राणी तीन गुणों से मुक्त नहीं। मुक्ति गुणों की अनुपस्थिति नहीं बल्कि उनके साथ तादात्म्य का अतिक्रमण है।

verses.sanskrit:
ब्राह्मणक्षत्रियविशां शूद्राणां च परन्तप |
कर्माणि प्रविभक्तानि स्वभावप्रभवैर्गुणैः ||४१||
verses.translation_en: Brahmanas, kshatriyas, vaishyas, and shudras are distinguished by the qualities born of their own natures in accordance with the material modes, O chastiser of the enemy.
verses.translation_hi: हे परन्तप! ब्राह्मण, क्षत्रिय, वैश्य और शूद्रों के कर्म स्वभाव से उत्पन्न गुणों द्वारा विभाजित हैं।
verses.meaning_en: Having established the universality of the gunas (verse 40), Krishna now applies the guna-framework to the social order. The four varnas — brahman (the learned, the teachers), kshatriya (the warriors, the rulers), vaishya (the traders, the farmers), and shudra (the artisans, the servants) — are distinguished by the qualities (gunas) born of their own nature (svabhava-prabhavaih). This is a crucial formulation: varna is not determined by birth alone but by svabhava (one's own nature) and the gunas that nature expresses. The social order, in the Gita's vision, is not a rigid hierarchy of intrinsic worth but a differentiation of function based on the guna-composition that each person has developed through their karma and choices. Each function is essential; each is sacred when performed as yoga.
verses.meaning_hi: गुणों की सार्वभौमिकता स्थापित करने के बाद कृष्ण गुण-ढाँचे को सामाजिक व्यवस्था पर लागू करते हैं। चार वर्ण — ब्राह्मण, क्षत्रिय, वैश्य और शूद्र — 'स्वभावप्रभवैर्गुणैः' — अपने स्वभाव से उत्पन्न गुणों से विभाजित। यह एक महत्वपूर्ण सूत्रीकरण है: वर्ण केवल जन्म से नहीं बल्कि स्वभाव और उसके गुणों से। प्रत्येक कार्य आवश्यक है; प्रत्येक योग के रूप में किए जाने पर पवित्र है।

verses.sanskrit:
शमो दमस्तपः शौचं क्षान्तिरार्जवमेव च |
ज्ञानं विज्ञानमास्तिक्यं ब्रह्मकर्म स्वभावजम् ||४२||
verses.translation_en: Peacefulness, self-control, austerity, purity, tolerance, honesty, knowledge, wisdom, and religiousness — these are the natural qualities by which the brahmanas work.
verses.translation_hi: शम, दम, तप, शौच, क्षान्ति, आर्जव, ज्ञान, विज्ञान और आस्तिक्य — ये ब्राह्मण के स्वभावज कर्म हैं।
verses.meaning_en: The nine qualities that constitute the natural functioning of the brahman-nature: shama (inner peace, the stillness of the controlled mind), dama (outer self-control, mastery over the senses), tapas (austerity — the discipline that purifies), shaucha (purity — inner and outer cleanliness), kshanti (patience, forbearance), arjava (straightforwardness, honesty), jnanam (knowledge — the theoretical understanding of reality), vijnanam (practical wisdom, the direct experiential insight that goes beyond theory), and astikyam (faith in the validity of the Vedas and the transcendent order). These nine qualities together constitute the svabhava of the brahman-nature: the natural orientation toward knowledge, inner refinement, and the transmission of wisdom. When these qualities are present, one is functionally a brahman regardless of birth.
verses.meaning_hi: ब्राह्मण-प्रकृति के स्वाभाविक कार्य के नौ गुण: शम (आंतरिक शांति), दम (बाह्य आत्म-नियंत्रण), तप, शौच, क्षान्ति, आर्जव, ज्ञान (सैद्धांतिक समझ), विज्ञान (प्रत्यक्ष अनुभवात्मक अंतर्दृष्टि जो सिद्धांत से परे है), और आस्तिक्य। जब ये गुण उपस्थित हों तो व्यक्ति कार्यात्मक रूप से ब्राह्मण है — जन्म से निरपेक्ष।

verses.sanskrit:
शौर्यं तेजो धृतिर्दाक्ष्यं युद्धे चाप्यपलायनम् |
दानमीश्वरभावश्च क्षात्रं कर्म स्वभावजम् ||४३||
verses.translation_en: Heroism, power, determination, resourcefulness, courage in battle, generosity, and leadership are the natural qualities of work for the kshatriyas.
verses.translation_hi: शौर्य, तेज, धृति, दाक्ष्य, युद्ध में न भागना, दान और ईश्वरभाव — ये क्षत्रिय के स्वभावज कर्म हैं।
verses.meaning_en: Seven qualities constitute the kshatriya-nature: shaurya (heroism, the courage to face danger), teja (power, vital force — the luminous energy of the noble warrior), dhrti (steadiness, determination under pressure), dakshya (resourcefulness, skill — the practical intelligence that finds solutions), 'yuddhe capy apalayam' (not fleeing from battle — the commitment to stand one's ground), danam (generosity — the open-handed giving of the protector), and ishvara-bhava (the quality of governance, of leadership, of the capacity to exercise authority with justice). The kshatriya-nature is the nature of the noble protector: strong, courageous, skilled, steady, generous, and capable of responsible authority. When Arjuna's bow trembles in Chapter 1, he is momentarily failing to embody his own svabhava.
verses.meaning_hi: क्षत्रिय-प्रकृति के सात गुण: शौर्य, तेज, धृति, दाक्ष्य, युद्ध में अपलायन, दान और ईश्वरभाव। क्षत्रिय-प्रकृति श्रेष्ठ रक्षक की प्रकृति है: शक्तिशाली, साहसी, कुशल, दृढ़, उदार और जिम्मेदार प्राधिकार के सक्षम। जब अध्याय 1 में अर्जुन का धनुष काँपता है तो वह क्षणभर के लिए अपना स्वभाव जीने में विफल हो रहा है।

verses.sanskrit:
कृषिगौरक्ष्यवाणिज्यं वैश्यकर्म स्वभावजम् |
परिचर्यात्मकं कर्म शूद्रस्यापि स्वभावजम् ||४४||
verses.translation_en: Farming, cow protection, and business are the natural work for the vaishyas, and for the shudras there is labor and service to others.
verses.translation_hi: कृषि, गोरक्षा और वाणिज्य वैश्य के स्वभावज कर्म हैं; और शूद्र का स्वभावज कर्म परिचर्यात्मक (सेवा) है।
verses.meaning_en: The vaishya-nature: 'krishi-gau-rakshya-vanijyam' — agriculture (krishi), cow protection (gau-rakshya — the care of the nourishing animals that sustain community life), and trade (vanijyam — commerce, the movement of goods). The vaishya-nature is the nature of the nourisher and the sustainer: the one who grows food, cares for animals, and maintains the flow of material goods through the community. The shudra-nature: 'paricharyatmakam karma' — service-oriented work (parichary — attending, caring for, serving). This includes artisanship, craft, and all the supporting work that makes civilized life possible. Every form of genuine service, when offered with the right inner orientation, is as noble and as liberating as any other form of action.
verses.meaning_hi: वैश्य-प्रकृति: 'कृषिगौरक्ष्यवाणिज्यम्' — कृषि, गोरक्षा और वाणिज्य। वैश्य-प्रकृति पोषण करने वाले और जीविका देने वाले की प्रकृति है। शूद्र-प्रकृति: 'परिचर्यात्मकं कर्म' — सेवाभावी कर्म। प्रत्येक प्रकार की वास्तविक सेवा, सही आंतरिक अभिमुखता के साथ अर्पित की जाए तो किसी भी अन्य प्रकार के कर्म जितनी श्रेष्ठ और मुक्तिदायी है।

verses.sanskrit:
स्वे स्वे कर्मण्यभिरतः संसिद्धिं लभते नरः |
स्वकर्मनिरतः सिद्धिं यथा विन्दति तच्छृणु ||४५||
verses.translation_en: By following his qualities of work, every man can become perfect. Now please hear from Me how this can be done.
verses.translation_hi: अपने-अपने कर्म में अभिरत होकर मनुष्य संसिद्धि प्राप्त करता है। स्वकर्म में निरत रहने वाला सिद्धि कैसे पाता है — यह सुनो।
verses.meaning_en: 'Sve sve karmany abhiratah samsiddhim labhate narah' — delighting in one's own particular work (sve sve karmani — one's own work, the work proper to one's nature), a person attains perfect fulfillment (samsiddhi). 'Svakarmaniratah siddhim yatha vindati tac chrinu' — how one who is engaged in one's own work finds siddhi (perfection, accomplishment) — hear that from Me. The promise is radical: any person, in any function, doing the work that arises from their own nature, can attain the highest perfection. It is not the category of the work but the quality of the engagement with it — the presence, the devotion, the offering — that determines whether work becomes yoga and yoga becomes liberation.
verses.meaning_hi: 'स्वे स्वे कर्मण्यभिरतः संसिद्धिं लभते नरः' — अपने-अपने कर्म में आनंद लेते हुए व्यक्ति परम सिद्धि पाता है। 'स्वकर्मनिरतः सिद्धिं यथा विन्दति' — स्वकर्म में निरत सिद्धि कैसे पाता है। वादा कट्टरपंथी है: कोई भी व्यक्ति, किसी भी कार्य में, अपनी प्रकृति से उत्पन्न कर्म करते हुए परम सिद्धि पा सकता है। कर्म की श्रेणी नहीं बल्कि उसके साथ संलग्नता की गुणवत्ता महत्वपूर्ण है।

verses.sanskrit:
यतः प्रवृत्तिर्भूतानां येन सर्वमिदं ततम् |
स्वकर्मणा तमभ्यर्च्य सिद्धिं विन्दति मानवः ||४६||
verses.translation_en: By worship of the Lord, who is the source of all beings and who is all-pervading, a man can attain perfection through performing his own work.
verses.translation_hi: जिससे सभी प्राणियों की प्रवृत्ति है और जिससे यह सब व्याप्त है — उसे स्वकर्म द्वारा पूजकर मनुष्य सिद्धि पाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Yatah pravrittir bhutanam yena sarvam idam tatam' — from whom is the activity of all beings (pravritti — the impetus, the movement, the life-force of all existence), by whom all this is pervaded (tatam — spread through, permeating). 'Svakarmana tam abhyarcya siddhim vindati manavah' — worshipping That through one's own work (svakarmana — by means of one's own particular work), a human being finds perfection. This is the culmination of the karma-yoga teaching: every act of genuine, selfless work in one's proper function becomes an act of worship of the Divine. The farmer's tending of the soil, the warrior's protection of the innocent, the teacher's transmission of wisdom — when done as offering to That from which all life flows, every act becomes sacred and every act purifies.
verses.meaning_hi: 'यतः प्रवृत्तिर्भूतानां येन सर्वमिदं ततम्' — जिससे सभी की प्रवृत्ति और जिससे यह सब व्याप्त। 'स्वकर्मणा तमभ्यर्च्य सिद्धिं विन्दति मानवः' — स्वकर्म द्वारा उसकी पूजा करके सिद्धि पाता है। यह कर्मयोग शिक्षा का शिखर है: अपनी प्रकृति में निःस्वार्थ, वास्तविक कर्म का प्रत्येक कार्य उस दिव्य की पूजा बन जाता है जिससे समस्त जीवन प्रवाहित होता है।

verses.sanskrit:
श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात् |
स्वभावनियतं कर्म कुर्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम् ||४७||
verses.translation_en: It is better to engage in one's own occupation, even though one may perform it imperfectly, than to accept another's occupation and perform it perfectly. Duties prescribed according to one's nature are never affected by sinful reactions.
verses.translation_hi: स्वधर्म विगुण भी परधर्म से जो उत्तम अनुष्ठित हो — श्रेयस्कर है। स्वभावनियत कर्म करने से पाप नहीं लगता।
verses.meaning_en: 'Shreyaan svadharmo vigunah paradharmat svanushthitat' — one's own dharma (svadharma), even if imperfectly performed (vigunah — having flaws, deficiencies), is better (shreyaan) than the dharma of another (paradharma) even perfectly performed (svanu-shthitat — well accomplished). 'Svabhava-niyatam karma kurvan napnoti kilbisham' — performing action regulated by one's own nature (svabhava-niyatam), one incurs no sin (kilbisha). This teaching has been given before (Chapter 3, verse 35) and is repeated here near the end of the Gita for emphasis: the path of authentic alignment with one's own nature — even imperfect — is infinitely better than the path of imitation, however polished. The imitation of another's dharma, however impressive, is ultimately empty; the authentic expression of one's own nature, even rough-edged, carries the seed of liberation.
verses.meaning_hi: 'श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात्' — अपना धर्म, भले ही त्रुटिपूर्ण हो, दूसरे के सुनिष्पादित धर्म से श्रेयस्कर है। 'स्वभावनियतं कर्म कुर्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम्' — स्वभाव से नियत कर्म करने से पाप नहीं। यह शिक्षा अध्याय 3 में भी थी और यहाँ फिर आती है: प्रामाणिक स्वभाव-संरेखण — भले ही अपूर्ण — नकल से अनंत गुना बेहतर।

verses.sanskrit:
सहजं कर्म कौन्तेय सदोषमपि न त्यजेत् |
सर्वारम्भा हि दोषेण धूमेनाग्निरिवावृताः ||४८||
verses.translation_en: Every endeavor is covered by some fault, just as fire is covered by smoke. Therefore one should not give up the work born of his nature, O son of Kunti, even if such work is full of fault.
verses.translation_hi: हे कुन्तीपुत्र! सहज कर्म दोषयुक्त हो तो भी नहीं त्यागना चाहिए — क्योंकि सभी आरंभ धुएँ से अग्नि की तरह दोष से आवृत हैं।
verses.meaning_en: 'Sahajaṃ karma Kaunteya sadosham api na tyajet' — one's natural (sahaja — born together with, innate) work should not be abandoned, O son of Kunti, even if it is full of fault (sadosham). 'Sarvarambha hi doshena dhumenaagnir ivavritah' — for all undertakings (sarvarambha) are covered (avritah) by fault (dosha) as fire is covered by smoke. The analogy is perfect: fire always produces smoke; you cannot have the fire without the smoke. Every action has its shadow, its limitation, its imperfection, its unintended consequence. The warrior's protection involves harm; the teacher's wisdom contains error; the farmer's cultivation disturbs the earth. None of this justifies abandoning the work. The smoke is not a reason to extinguish the fire but an unavoidable condition of the fire's existence.
verses.meaning_hi: 'सहजं कर्म कौन्तेय सदोषमपि न त्यजेत्' — सहज कर्म, दोषयुक्त हो तो भी न छोड़ें। 'सर्वारम्भा हि दोषेण धूमेनाग्निरिवावृताः' — जैसे अग्नि धुएँ से, सभी आरंभ दोष से आवृत। उपमा परिपूर्ण है: अग्नि हमेशा धुआँ पैदा करती है; बिना धुएँ के अग्नि नहीं। धुआँ अग्नि बुझाने का कारण नहीं बल्कि उसके अस्तित्व की अनिवार्य स्थिति है।

verses.sanskrit:
असक्तबुद्धिः सर्वत्र जितात्मा विगतस्पृहः |
नैष्कर्म्यसिद्धिं परमां संन्यासेनाधिगच्छति ||४९||
verses.translation_en: One who is self-controlled and unattached and who disregards all material enjoyments can obtain, by practice of renunciation, the highest perfect stage of freedom from reaction.
verses.translation_hi: सर्वत्र असक्तबुद्धि, जितात्मा और विगतस्पृह व्यक्ति संन्यास से परम नैष्कर्म्यसिद्धि प्राप्त करता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Asakta-buddhih sarvatra jitatma vigata-sprihah' — one whose intelligence is unattached everywhere (asakta-buddhi — the intelligence that does not cling to any object or outcome), who has conquered the self (jitatma — mastery over the inner instruments), who is free from craving (vigata-spriha — desire has departed). 'Naishkarmya-siddhim paramam sannyasenadhigacchati' — such a person attains through sannyas the supreme perfection of naishkarmya (naishkarmya-siddhi — the perfection of action-beyond-karma, the state in which action is performed but karma is not accumulated). This is not the withdrawal from action but the inner freedom within action: acting fully, with all the faculties engaged, and yet with the freedom of one who knows that the Self neither acts nor accumulates.
verses.meaning_hi: 'असक्तबुद्धिः सर्वत्र जितात्मा विगतस्पृहः' — सर्वत्र असक्त बुद्धि, जितात्मा, विगतस्पृह। 'नैष्कर्म्यसिद्धिं परमां संन्यासेनाधिगच्छति' — संन्यास से परम नैष्कर्म्यसिद्धि। यह कर्म से निवृत्ति नहीं बल्कि कर्म के भीतर आंतरिक स्वतंत्रता है: पूरी शक्ति से कर्म करना, फिर भी उसकी स्वतंत्रता के साथ जो जानता है कि आत्मा न कर्म करती है न संचित करती है।

verses.sanskrit:
सिद्धिं प्राप्तो यथा ब्रह्म तथाप्नोति निबोध मे |
समासेनैव कौन्तेय निष्ठा ज्ञानस्य या परा ||५०||
verses.translation_en: O son of Kunti, learn from Me how one who has attained such perfection can attain to the supreme state, Brahman, the state of highest knowledge, by acting in the way I shall now summarize.
verses.translation_hi: हे कुन्तीपुत्र! सिद्धि प्राप्त व्यक्ति ब्रह्म को कैसे प्राप्त होता है — ज्ञान की परम निष्ठा संक्षेप में मुझसे समझो।
verses.meaning_en: 'Siddhim prapto yatha brahma tathapnoti nibodha me' — understand from Me how one who has attained siddhi (the perfection of naishkarmya described in verse 49) then attains Brahman (the Absolute). 'Samasenaiva Kaunteya nishtha jnanasya ya para' — in brief, O son of Kunti, the highest stability (para nishtha — the supreme establishment) of knowledge. The chapter now moves toward its culminating movement: having described the path of action and the qualities of the different dimensions of human life (knowledge, action, doer, intelligence, steadiness, happiness), Krishna now describes the final ascent to Brahman — the path from siddhi to the Absolute itself.
verses.meaning_hi: 'सिद्धिं प्राप्तो यथा ब्रह्म तथाप्नोति निबोध मे' — सिद्धिप्राप्त ब्रह्म को कैसे पाता है। 'ज्ञानस्य या परा निष्ठा' — ज्ञान की परम निष्ठा। अध्याय अब अपनी चरम गति की ओर बढ़ता है: कर्म के मार्ग और मानव जीवन के विभिन्न आयामों का वर्णन करने के बाद, कृष्ण ब्रह्म तक का अंतिम आरोहण वर्णित करते हैं।

verses.sanskrit:
बुद्ध्या विशुद्धया युक्तो धृत्यात्मानं नियम्य च |
शब्दादीन्विषयांस्त्यक्त्वा रागद्वेषौ व्युदस्य च ||५१||
verses.translation_en: Being purified by his intelligence and controlling the mind with determination, giving up the objects of sense gratification, being freed from attachment and hatred —
verses.translation_hi: विशुद्ध बुद्धि से युक्त, धृति द्वारा आत्मा को नियमित करके, शब्दादि विषयों को त्यागकर और राग-द्वेष को दूर करके —
verses.meaning_en: Verses 51, 52, and 53 together describe the ascending preparation for the direct realization of Brahman. The qualities of this ascent are cumulative: 'Buddhya vishuddhaya yukto' — joined with purified (vishuddha — thoroughly cleansed) intelligence. 'Dhritya atmanam niyamya ca' — controlling the self through steadiness (dhrti). 'Shabdadin vishayan tyaktva' — having abandoned the sense objects (shabda, sound, and the other sense-objects). 'Raga-dveshau vyudasya ca' — having set aside (vyudasya — pushed away, cast off) attraction (raga) and aversion (dvesha). These four qualities of the preparation: purified intelligence, self-regulation through steadiness, abandonment of sense-objects, and freedom from the reactive pulls of like and dislike.
verses.meaning_hi: श्लोक 51, 52 और 53 मिलकर ब्रह्म के प्रत्यक्ष साक्षात्कार की आरोही तैयारी का वर्णन करते हैं: 'बुद्ध्या विशुद्धया युक्तः' — शुद्ध बुद्धि से युक्त। 'धृत्यात्मानं नियम्य' — धृति द्वारा आत्मा को नियमित। 'शब्दादीन्विषयांस्त्यक्त्वा' — इन्द्रिय-विषयों का त्याग। 'रागद्वेषौ व्युदस्य' — राग-द्वेष को दूर करके।

verses.sanskrit:
विविक्तसेवी लघ्वाशी यतवाक्कायमानसः |
ध्यानयोगपरो नित्यं वैराग्यं समुपाश्रितः ||५२||
verses.translation_en: One who lives in a secluded place, who eats little, who controls his bodily, mental, and vocal activities, who is always in trance and who is detached, who is freed from false ego, false strength, false pride, lust, anger, and who does not accept things, being freed from the sense of ownership, is certainly elevated to the position of self-realization.
verses.translation_hi: विविक्त स्थान सेवन, लघु आहार, यतवाक्कायमानस, नित्य ध्यानयोगपर और वैराग्य को आश्रय देने वाला —
verses.meaning_en: 'Vivikta-sevi' — frequenting solitary places (the inner and outer retreat from the noise of the world). 'Laghv-ashi' — eating lightly (the reduction of sensory input and the burden on the digestive system, allowing greater clarity). 'Yata-vak-kaya-manasah' — with controlled speech (vak), body (kaya), and mind (manasah). 'Dhyana-yoga-parah nityam' — always established in the highest practice of dhyana-yoga (the direct inward practice of meditation). 'Vairagyam samupashritah' — taking refuge in vairagya (the natural dispassion that arises from having seen through the impermanence of all conditioned pleasures). Each quality builds on the previous: solitude, light eating, and control of body-speech-mind create the conditions for deep meditation; deep meditation cultivates the natural vairagya that is not forced renunciation but spontaneous freedom.
verses.meaning_hi: 'विविक्तसेवी' — एकांत स्थान सेवन। 'लघ्वाशी' — हल्का भोजन। 'यतवाक्कायमानसः' — नियंत्रित वाणी, शरीर और मन। 'ध्यानयोगपरो नित्यम्' — सदा ध्यानयोग में स्थित। 'वैराग्यं समुपाश्रितः' — वैराग्य का आश्रय। प्रत्येक गुण पिछले पर निर्मित: एकांत, हल्का भोजन, और त्रिविध नियंत्रण गहरे ध्यान की स्थिति बनाते हैं।

verses.sanskrit:
अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं परिग्रहम् |
विमुच्य निर्ममः शान्तो ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ||५३||
verses.translation_en: Being freed from attachment, fear, and anger, being fully absorbed in Me and taking refuge in Me, many, many persons in the past became purified by knowledge of Me — and thus they all attained transcendental love for Me.
verses.translation_hi: अहंकार, बल, दर्प, काम, क्रोध और परिग्रह से विमुक्त होकर, निर्मम और शांत व्यक्ति ब्रह्मभाव के योग्य होता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Ahankaram balam darpam kamam krodhm parigraham' — having released (vimucya — freed oneself from) the ego (ahankara), strength used as power-over (balam — force), arrogance (darpa), desire (kama), anger (krodha), and possessiveness (parigraha — the accumulation and hoarding instinct). 'Nirmamah shanto brahma-bhuyaya kalpate' — free from 'mine' (nirmama — without the mine-sense), peaceful (shanta), such a person becomes qualified for Brahman-realization (brahma-bhuyaya kalpate — fit for becoming Brahman). Six qualities must be released: ego, domineering force, arrogance, desire, anger, and possessiveness. When these six are genuinely dissolved — not suppressed but seen through and released — what remains is the quiet, open, spacious awareness that is already Brahman.
verses.meaning_hi: 'अहंकारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं परिग्रहम्' — अहंकार, बल, दर्प, काम, क्रोध और परिग्रह से विमुक्त। 'निर्ममः शान्तः ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते' — निर्मम और शांत व्यक्ति ब्रह्मभाव का योग्य। छह गुण मुक्त होने चाहिए। जब ये छह वास्तव में विघटित हों तो जो शेष रहता है वह शांत, खुली जागरूकता है जो पहले से ही ब्रह्म है।

verses.sanskrit:
ब्रह्मभूतः प्रसन्नात्मा न शोचति न काङ्क्षति |
समः सर्वेषु भूतेषु मद्भक्तिं लभते पराम् ||५४||
verses.translation_en: One who is thus transcendentally situated at once realizes the Supreme Brahman and becomes fully joyful. He never laments or desires to have anything. He is equally disposed toward every living entity. In that state he attains pure devotional service unto Me.
verses.translation_hi: ब्रह्मभूत होकर प्रसन्नात्मा, न शोक करता है, न आकांक्षा — सभी भूतों में सम होकर मेरी परम भक्ति पाता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Brahma-bhutah prasannatma na shocati na kanksati' — one who has become Brahman (brahma-bhutah — established in Brahman-consciousness), with a serene Self (prasannatma — the Self that is settled in its own luminosity), neither grieves (na shocati) nor desires (na kanksati). 'Samah sarveshu bhuteshu mad-bhaktim labhate param' — equal toward all beings (samah sarveshu bhuteshu), that person attains My supreme devotion (param bhakti). This is one of the most important verses in the Gita: Brahman-realization does not lead to impersonal absorption but to the flowering of supreme devotion. The path to the Personal Divine (bhakti) passes through the impersonal (Brahman). Once the ego-self has dissolved into the recognition of Brahman, what remains naturally turns toward the Personal — toward the relationship with the Divine that is not possession but pure love.
verses.meaning_hi: 'ब्रह्मभूतः प्रसन्नात्मा न शोचति न कांक्षति' — ब्रह्मभूत, प्रसन्नात्मा, न शोक न आकांक्षा। 'समः सर्वेषु भूतेषु मद्भक्तिं लभते पराम्' — सभी भूतों में सम होकर परम भक्ति। गीता के सबसे महत्वपूर्ण श्लोकों में से एक: ब्रह्म-साक्षात्कार निर्वैयक्तिक अवशोषण नहीं बल्कि परम भक्ति की पुष्पावस्था है। व्यक्तिगत दिव्य (भक्ति) का मार्ग निर्वैयक्तिक (ब्रह्म) से होकर जाता है।

verses.sanskrit:
भक्त्या मामभिजानाति यावान्यश्चास्मि तत्त्वतः |
ततो मां तत्त्वतो ज्ञात्वा विशते तदनन्तरम् ||५५||
verses.translation_en: One can understand Me as I am, as the Supreme Personality of Godhead, only by devotional service. And when one is in full consciousness of Me by such devotion, he can enter into the kingdom of God.
verses.translation_hi: भक्ति द्वारा वह मुझे यावान और जो मैं हूँ तत्त्वतः जानता है — फिर तत्त्वतः जानकर तदनंतर मुझमें प्रवेश करता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Bhaktya mam abhijanati yavan yash casmi tattvatah' — through devotion (bhaktya) one knows Me (abhijanati) as I truly am (tattvatah — in my reality, in my truth) — yavan (in My full extent) and yah (what I am). 'Tato mam tattvatah jnatva vishate tad anantaram' — then, having known Me in truth, one enters into Me immediately after (tad anantaram). The supreme claim: the Absolute as It truly is — not just the impersonal Brahman, not just the philosophical concept, but the living, personal Reality in its full depth — is known only through bhakti. Knowledge can take you to the threshold; it is only love that enters. And the entrance is immediate — 'tad anantaram' — right after genuine knowing, there is entering, there is union.
verses.meaning_hi: 'भक्त्या मामभिजानाति यावान्यश्चास्मि तत्त्वतः' — भक्ति द्वारा मुझे तत्त्वतः जानता है। 'विशते तदनन्तरम्' — तत्काल मुझमें प्रवेश करता है। सर्वोच्च दावा: परम को जैसा वह वास्तव में है — केवल भक्ति से जाना जाता है। ज्ञान सीमा तक ले जा सकता है; केवल प्रेम प्रवेश करता है। और प्रवेश तत्काल है।

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वकर्माण्यपि सदा कुर्वाणो मद्व्यपाश्रयः |
मत्प्रसादादवाप्नोति शाश्वतं पदमव्ययम् ||५६||
verses.translation_en: Though engaged in all kinds of activities, My pure devotee, under My protection, reaches the eternal and imperishable abode by My grace.
verses.translation_hi: सदा सभी कर्म करते हुए भी मेरे व्यपाश्रय (शरण) वाला मेरी कृपा से शाश्वत और अव्यय पद को प्राप्त होता है।
verses.meaning_en: 'Sarva-karmany api sada kurva-no mad-vyapashrayah' — performing all activities always (sarva-karmany — all actions, without exception; sada — always), taking refuge in Me (mad-vyapashrayah — under My shelter, My protection). 'Mat-prasadad avapnoti shashvatam padam avyayam' — by My grace (mat-prasadat — through My blessing, which flows freely to those who take shelter in Me), one attains the eternal (shashvatam) and imperishable (avyayam) state. All actions — not selected spiritual actions but all actions — performed in the shelter of the Divine lead to the eternal state. This is the practical summation: there is no action too ordinary to be offered, no function too humble to be consecrated. The entirety of life, lived in the shelter of the Divine, becomes the path.
verses.meaning_hi: 'सर्वकर्माण्यपि सदा कुर्वाणो मद्व्यपाश्रयः' — सभी कर्म सदा करते हुए, मेरे व्यपाश्रय में। 'मत्प्रसादादवाप्नोति शाश्वतं पदमव्ययम्' — मेरी कृपा से शाश्वत और अव्यय पद। सभी कर्म — चुनिंदा आध्यात्मिक कर्म नहीं बल्कि सभी कर्म — दिव्य की शरण में किए जाने पर शाश्वत अवस्था की ओर ले जाते हैं। जीवन की समग्रता, दिव्य की शरण में जीते हुए, मार्ग बन जाती है।

verses.sanskrit:
चेतसा सर्वकर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्परः |
बुद्धियोगमुपाश्रित्य मच्चित्तः सततं भव ||५७||
verses.translation_en: In all activities just depend upon Me and work always under My protection. In such devotional service, be fully conscious of Me.
verses.translation_hi: चेतस् से सभी कर्मों को मुझमें संन्यस्त करके, मत्पर होकर, बुद्धियोग का आश्रय लेकर सदा मच्चित्त हो।
verses.meaning_en: 'Cetasa sarva-karmani mayi sannyasya mat-parah' — with the mind (cetasa), renouncing (sannyasya — surrendering) all actions into Me, with Me as the supreme (mat-parah). 'Buddhi-yogam upashritya mac-cittah satatam bhava' — taking refuge in buddhi-yoga (the yoga of purified intelligence), be always (satatam — continuously) absorbed in Me (mac-cittah — your mind in Me). The instruction is both total and continuous: not 'think of Me occasionally' but 'be always absorbed in Me.' The practical method: sannyasa of actions into the Divine (surrender of doership) + refuge in buddhi-yoga (the purified intelligence that discriminates the Real from the unreal) + constant absorption in the Divine presence. This is the integration of all the chapter's teachings into a single, continuous, livable practice.
verses.meaning_hi: 'चेतसा सर्वकर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्परः' — चेतस से सभी कर्म मुझमें संन्यस्त करके, मत्पर होकर। 'बुद्धियोगमुपाश्रित्य मच्चित्तः सततं भव' — बुद्धियोग का आश्रय लेकर सदा मच्चित्त हो। निर्देश एक साथ कुल और निरंतर: 'सदा मुझमें अवशोषित हो।' यह अध्याय की सभी शिक्षाओं का एक निरंतर, जीने योग्य अभ्यास में एकीकरण है।

verses.sanskrit:
मच्चित्तः सर्वदुर्गाणि मत्प्रसादात्तरिष्यसि |
अथ चेत्त्वमहङ्कारान्न श्रोष्यसि विनङ्क्ष्यसि ||५८||
verses.translation_en: If you become conscious of Me, you will pass over all the obstacles of conditioned life by My grace. If, however, you do not work in such consciousness but act through false ego, not hearing Me, you will be lost.
verses.translation_hi: मच्चित्त होने से मेरी कृपा से सभी दुर्गमताओं को पार करोगे। किंतु यदि अहंकार से नहीं सुनोगे तो नष्ट हो जाओगे।
verses.meaning_en: 'Mac-cittah sarva-durgani mat-prasadat tarishyasi' — absorbed in Me (mac-cittah), you will cross over (tarishyasi) all difficulties and obstacles (sarva-durgani — all the inaccessible, difficult places) by My grace (mat-prasadat). 'Atha cet tvam ahankaran na shroshyasi vinankshyasi' — but if, because of ego (ahankarat — due to the ego's insistence on its own authority), you do not hear (na shroshyasi — will not listen, will not receive), you will be destroyed (vinankshyasi — you will perish). The contrast is stark: with the Divine as one's center, all obstacles can be crossed by grace. With the ego as one's center, refusing to hear, refusing to surrender, even the person's natural capacities become instruments of self-destruction.
verses.meaning_hi: 'मच्चित्तः सर्वदुर्गाणि मत्प्रसादात्तरिष्यसि' — मच्चित्त होने से कृपा से सभी कठिनाइयाँ पार। 'अथ चेत्त्वमहंकारान्न श्रोष्यसि विनंक्ष्यसि' — किंतु अहंकार से न सुनोगे तो नष्ट। विपरीतता तीखी है: दिव्य को केंद्र में रखने से कृपा से सभी बाधाएँ पार। अहंकार को केंद्र में रखने से — स्वयं की क्षमताएँ भी आत्म-विनाश के उपकरण बन जाती हैं।

verses.sanskrit:
यदहङ्कारमाश्रित्य न योत्स्य इति मन्यसे |
मिथ्यैष व्यवसायस्ते प्रकृतिस्त्वां नियोक्ष्यति ||५९||
verses.translation_en: If you do not act according to My direction and do not fight, then you will be falsely directed. By your nature, you will have to be engaged in warfare.
verses.translation_hi: यदि अहंकार का आश्रय लेकर सोचते हो 'मैं नहीं लड़ूँगा' — तो यह संकल्प मिथ्या है; प्रकृति तुम्हें नियुक्त करेगी।
verses.meaning_en: 'Yad ahankaram ashritya na yotsya iti manyase' — if, taking refuge in ego (the false ego's decision-making authority), you think 'I will not fight' (na yotsya — I will not engage in this battle). 'Mithyaisha vyavasayas te' — this resolution (vyavasaya — the firm decision, the determined intention) of yours is false (mithya). 'Prakritish tvam niyokshyati' — your own nature (prakriti — both the cosmic nature and your own svabhava, your own deeply ingrained warrior-nature) will press you into action. This is a profound psychological observation: the ego can make declarations all it wants, but the deeper forces of one's own nature — the svabhava shaped by centuries of karma and training — will ultimately override the ego's resistance. What you are made of will express itself.
verses.meaning_hi: 'यदहंकारमाश्रित्य न योत्स्य इति मन्यसे' — अहंकार का आश्रय लेकर सोचते हो 'नहीं लड़ूँगा'। 'मिथ्यैष व्यवसायस्ते' — यह संकल्प मिथ्या है। 'प्रकृतिस्त्वां नियोक्ष्यति' — प्रकृति नियुक्त करेगी। गहरी मनोवैज्ञानिक टिप्पणी: अहंकार जो चाहे घोषणाएँ करे, लेकिन अपनी प्रकृति की गहरी शक्तियाँ अंततः अहंकार के प्रतिरोध को ओवरराइड करेंगी।

verses.sanskrit:
स्वभावजेन कौन्तेय निबद्धः स्वेन कर्मणा |
कर्तुं नेच्छसि यन्मोहात्करिष्यस्यवशोऽपि तत् ||६०||
verses.translation_en: Under illusion you are now declining to act according to My direction. But, compelled by the work born of your own nature, you will act all the same, O son of Kunti.
verses.translation_hi: हे कुन्तीपुत्र! स्वभावज कर्म से बँधे हुए, मोह से जो करना नहीं चाहते — वह अवश होकर भी करोगे।
verses.meaning_en: 'Svabhavjena Kaunteya nibaddha svena karmana' — O son of Kunti, bound (nibaddha) by your own action arising from your own nature (svabhava-jena svena karmana). 'Kartum necchasi yan mohat' — that which, out of delusion (mohat), you do not wish to do. 'Karishyasy avasho'pi tat' — you will do that even against your will (avasho — without will, helplessly, inevitably). The deepest teaching about the relationship between will and nature: what we have made ourselves into through long practice and accumulated karma has a momentum that the momentary decision of the ego cannot simply override. This is both humbling and liberating: humbling because it shows the limits of willful self-invention; liberating because it means that the genuine transformation of one's svabhava is the most powerful force available.
verses.meaning_hi: 'स्वभावजेन कौन्तेय निबद्धः स्वेन कर्मणा' — अपने स्वभावज कर्म से बँधे। 'कर्तुं नेच्छसि यन्मोहात्करिष्यस्यवशोऽपि तत्' — मोह से जो नहीं चाहते, अवश होकर भी करोगे। इच्छाशक्ति और प्रकृति के संबंध के बारे में गहरी शिक्षा: हमने जो स्वयं को बनाया है उसमें एक गति है जिसे अहंकार का क्षणिक निर्णय नहीं ओवरराइड कर सकता।

verses.sanskrit:
ईश्वरः सर्वभूतानां हृद्देशेऽर्जुन तिष्ठति |
भ्रामयन्सर्वभूतानि यन्त्रारूढानि मायया ||६१||
verses.translation_en: The Supreme Lord is situated in everyone's heart, O Arjuna, and is directing the wanderings of all living entities, who are seated as on a machine, made of the material energy.
verses.translation_hi: हे अर्जुन! ईश्वर सभी प्राणियों के हृदय में स्थित है — माया द्वारा यंत्रारूढ़ सभी प्राणियों को भ्रमाता हुआ।
verses.meaning_en: 'Ishvarah sarva-bhutanam hrid-deshe'rjuna tishthati' — the Lord (Ishvara) dwells (tishthati) in the heart-region (hrid-deshe) of all beings (sarva-bhutanam), O Arjuna. 'Bhramayan sarva-bhutani yantrarudhani mayaya' — causing all beings to wander (bhramayan — revolving, spinning, directing through their journeys), as if mounted on a machine (yantra-rudhani — seated in/on a machine, a mechanism), by maya (the creative power of the Divine that makes the manifest world appear as it does). The image is precise and arresting: every living being is like a figure seated on a mechanical vehicle, and the Divine — present in the heart — is both the driver and the animating power. The 'wandering' is not purposeless; it is the journey of the soul toward the recognition of the Presence that has been guiding it all along.
verses.meaning_hi: 'ईश्वरः सर्वभूतानां हृद्देशेऽर्जुन तिष्ठति' — ईश्वर सभी प्राणियों के हृदय में स्थित है। 'भ्रामयन्सर्वभूतानि यन्त्रारूढानि मायया' — माया द्वारा यंत्रारूढ़ सभी को भ्रमाता हुआ। छवि सटीक और आकर्षक: प्रत्येक प्राणी एक यांत्रिक वाहन पर बैठी आकृति जैसा, और दिव्य — हृदय में उपस्थित — चालक और जीवनदायी शक्ति दोनों है।

verses.sanskrit:
तमेव शरणं गच्छ सर्वभावेन भारत |
तत्प्रसादात्परां शान्तिं स्थानं प्राप्स्यसि शाश्वतम् ||६२||
verses.translation_en: O scion of Bharata, surrender unto Him utterly. By His grace you will attain transcendental peace and the supreme and eternal abode.
verses.translation_hi: हे भारत! उसी की शरण में सर्वभाव से जाओ — उसकी कृपा से परम शांति और शाश्वत स्थान प्राप्त करोगे।
verses.meaning_en: 'Tam eva sharanam gaccha sarva-bhavena Bharata' — go to that One alone as your refuge, with your entire being (sarva-bhavena — with all modes of being, completely, holding nothing back), O scion of Bharata. 'Tat-prasadat param shantim sthanam prapsyasi shashvatam' — by His grace (tat-prasadat), you will attain supreme peace (param shantim) and the eternal abode (sthanam shashvatam). The teaching of surrender is unequivocal: total surrender (sarva-bhavena — not partial, not strategic, not with reservations) to the Divine who dwells in the heart. The result: not just peace but 'param shantim' — the ultimate peace that passes understanding, and the 'shashvatam sthanam' — the eternal abode that is beyond all the instability of the conditioned world.
verses.meaning_hi: 'तमेव शरणं गच्छ सर्वभावेन भारत' — उसी की शरण में सर्वभाव से जाओ। 'तत्प्रसादात्परां शान्तिं स्थानं प्राप्स्यसि शाश्वतम्' — उसकी कृपा से परम शांति और शाश्वत स्थान। समर्पण की शिक्षा स्पष्ट: पूर्ण समर्पण — आधा नहीं, रणनीतिक नहीं, आरक्षण के बिना — हृदय में निवास करने वाले दिव्य को।

verses.sanskrit:
इति ते ज्ञानमाख्यातं गुह्याद्गुह्यतरं मया |
विमृश्यैतदशेषेण यथेच्छसि तथा कुरु ||६३||
verses.translation_en: Thus I have explained to you knowledge still more confidential. Deliberate on this fully, and then do what you wish to do.
verses.translation_hi: इस प्रकार मैंने तुम्हें गुह्य से भी गुह्यतर ज्ञान सुनाया। इसे अशेष रूप से विचार करके जो इच्छा हो वह करो।
verses.meaning_en: 'Iti te jnanam akhyatam guhyad guhyataram maya' — thus has been declared to you by Me the knowledge that is more secret than the secret (guhyad guhyataram — the more hidden of the hidden). 'Vimrishya etad asheshena yathecchasi tatha kuru' — having deliberated on this fully and completely (vimrishya — reflecting upon, thinking through; asheshena — without remainder), do as you wish (yathecchasi tatha kuru). This is one of the most extraordinary moments in the Gita: after seventeen and a half chapters of teaching, Krishna does not issue a command but returns the choice completely to Arjuna. 'Do as you wish.' The teaching is offered as a gift, not imposed as a compulsion. Liberation is the freedom of the fully-informed choice, not the compliance of the compelled.
verses.meaning_hi: 'इति ते ज्ञानमाख्यातं गुह्याद्गुह्यतरं मया' — मैंने गुह्य से भी गुह्यतर ज्ञान सुनाया। 'विमृश्यैतदशेषेण यथेच्छसि तथा कुरु' — अशेष विचार करके जो इच्छा हो करो। गीता के सबसे असाधारण क्षणों में से एक: सत्रह से अधिक अध्यायों की शिक्षा के बाद, कृष्ण आदेश नहीं देते बल्कि चुनाव पूरी तरह अर्जुन को वापस करते हैं। शिक्षा उपहार के रूप में दी गई है, बाध्यता के रूप में नहीं।

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वगुह्यतमं भूयः शृणु मे परमं वचः |
इष्टोऽसि मे दृढमिति ततो वक्ष्यामि ते हितम् ||६४||
verses.translation_en: Because you are My very dear friend, I am speaking to you My supreme instruction, the most confidential knowledge of all. Hear this from Me, for it is for your benefit.
verses.translation_hi: सर्वगुह्यतम परम वचन फिर सुनो — तुम मुझे अत्यंत प्रिय हो, इसलिए तुम्हारा हित बताऊँगा।
verses.meaning_en: 'Sarva-guhyatamam bhuyah shrinu me paramam vacah' — hear again My supreme word, the most secret of all secrets (sarva-guhyatamam — the most hidden of everything hidden). 'Ishto'si me dridhnam iti tato vakshyami te hitam' — because you are firmly (dridhnam — with firmness, with certainty) dear to Me (ishto'si — you are beloved), therefore I will speak what is for your good (te hitam). After offering freedom of choice in verse 63, Krishna draws Arjuna back with the language of love: 'you are My very dear friend.' What follows is not a new argument but the final opening of the heart. The supreme secret is not held back by logic or earned by philosophical achievement — it is spoken because of love. The most secret teaching is the teaching of love itself.
verses.meaning_hi: 'सर्वगुह्यतमं भूयः शृणु मे परमं वचः' — सर्वगुह्यतम परम वचन फिर सुनो। 'इष्टोऽसि मे दृढमिति ततो वक्ष्यामि ते हितम्' — तुम मुझे दृढ़तापूर्वक प्रिय हो, इसलिए हित बताऊँगा। श्लोक 63 में स्वतंत्र चुनाव की पेशकश के बाद कृष्ण प्रेम की भाषा से अर्जुन को वापस खींचते हैं: 'तुम मुझे अत्यंत प्रिय हो।' जो आगे है वह नया तर्क नहीं बल्कि हृदय का अंतिम उद्घाटन है।

verses.sanskrit:
मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु |
मामेवैष्यसि सत्यं ते प्रतिजाने प्रियोऽसि मे ||६५||
verses.translation_en: Always think of Me, become My devotee, worship Me, and offer your homage unto Me. Thus you will come to Me without fail. I promise you this because you are My very dear friend.
verses.translation_hi: मन्मना हो, मेरा भक्त हो, मेरा पूजक हो, मुझे नमस्कार करो — तुम मुझे ही आओगे। सत्य प्रतिज्ञा करता हूँ — तुम मुझे प्रिय हो।
verses.meaning_en: 'Man-mana bhava mad-bhakto mad-yaji mam namaskuru' — let your mind be on Me (man-mana — your mind absorbed in Me), be My devotee (mad-bhakto), be My worshipper (mad-yaji — offer yajna to Me), bow to Me (mam namaskuru). 'Mam evai-shyasi satyam te pratijane priyo'si me' — you will come to Me, truly (satyam — I speak truth). I promise you (pratijane — I pledge, I commit), you are dear to Me (priyo'si me). Four practices: mind on Me, devotion to Me, worship of Me, reverence to Me. And then Krishna's personal promise: 'I pledge, you will come to Me.' The Divine makes a personal promise. This is the intimacy of the supreme relationship: not a contract but a vow of love. The teacher's love for the student is the final guarantee of the teaching.
verses.meaning_hi: 'मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु' — मन्मना, भक्त, पूजक, नमस्कारी हो। 'मामेवैष्यसि सत्यं ते प्रतिजाने प्रियोऽसि मे' — तुम मुझे ही आओगे — सत्य प्रतिज्ञा करता हूँ — तुम प्रिय हो। चार अभ्यास: मन्मना, भक्ति, पूजा, नमस्कार। और कृष्ण का व्यक्तिगत वादा। दिव्य व्यक्तिगत प्रतिज्ञा करता है: यह अनुबंध नहीं बल्कि प्रेम की शपथ है।

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज |
अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः ||६६||
verses.translation_en: Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto Me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.
verses.translation_hi: सभी धर्मों को त्यागकर मेरी एक शरण में आओ — मैं तुम्हें सभी पापों से मुक्त करूँगा, शोक मत करो।
verses.meaning_en: 'Sarva-dharman parityajya mam ekam sharanam vraja' — abandoning all dharmas (sarva-dharman — all the various obligations, duties, religious observances, philosophical positions, ethical frameworks), come to Me alone (mam ekam) as your refuge (sharanam vraja). 'Aham tva sarva-papebhyo mokshayishyami ma shucah' — I will liberate you (mokshayishyami) from all sins (sarva-papebhyo), do not grieve (ma shucah). This is the Gita's supreme verse, the charama shloka, the final, complete teaching: surrender everything — not just the sinful actions but even the dharmic ones, not just the impure motivations but even the pure ones — and take refuge in Me alone. In this total surrender, all accumulated karma is dissolved by the grace of the Divine. The promise is unqualified and unconditional: not 'I will help you' but 'I will liberate you.'
verses.meaning_hi: 'सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज' — सभी धर्मों को छोड़कर मेरी एक शरण में आओ। 'अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः' — सभी पापों से मुक्त करूँगा, शोक मत करो। यह गीता का चरम श्लोक, चरमश्लोक, अंतिम पूर्ण शिक्षा: सब कुछ समर्पण करो — न केवल पापी कर्म बल्कि धर्मिक भी — और केवल मेरी शरण लो। वचन बिना शर्त: 'मोक्षयिष्यामि' — मुक्त करूँगा।

verses.sanskrit:
इदं ते नातपस्काय नाभक्ताय कदाचन |
न चाशुश्रूषवे वाच्यं न च मां योऽभ्यसूयति ||६७||
verses.translation_en: This confidential knowledge may never be explained to those who are not austere, or devoted, or engaged in devotional service, nor to one who is envious of Me.
verses.translation_hi: यह तपस्वी न हो उसे, भक्त न हो उसे, शुश्रूषा न करे उसे और जो मुझसे ईर्ष्या करे उसे — कभी न कहना।
verses.meaning_en: 'Idam te natapaskaya nabhaktaya kadacana' — this (idam — this supreme teaching) should never (kadacana — ever, under any circumstances) be communicated to one who is not austere (atapaskaya — not committed to any discipline, not engaged in any tapas), nor to one who is not devoted (nabhaktaya — without bhakti, without the orientation of love toward the Divine). 'Na cashushrusave vacyam na ca mam yo'bhyasuyati' — nor to one who does not wish to listen (ashushrusave — without the quality of receptive hearing, without the readiness to serve and learn), and not to one who is envious of Me (yo mam abhyasuyati — who looks at Me with malice or contempt). The four who should not receive this teaching: the undisciplined, the undevoted, the unreceptive, and the envious. The teaching is not esoteric in the sense of being deliberately hidden; it is simply that those who lack these four qualities cannot receive it — like seeds that cannot germinate in frozen ground.
verses.meaning_hi: 'इदं ते नातपस्काय नाभक्ताय कदाचन' — तपस्वी न हो, भक्त न हो उसे कभी न कहना। 'न चाशुश्रूषवे वाच्यं न च मां योऽभ्यसूयति' — न शुश्रूषु को, न ईर्ष्यालु को। जिनमें ये चार गुण न हों उन्हें यह शिक्षा नहीं: अनुशासनहीन, अभक्त, अग्राह्य और ईर्ष्यालु। शिक्षा जानबूझकर छुपाई नहीं बल्कि जमी हुई भूमि में बीज अंकुरित नहीं होते।

verses.sanskrit:
य इमं परमं गुह्यं मद्भक्तेष्वभिधास्यति |
भक्तिं मयि परां कृत्वा मामेवैष्यत्यसंशयः ||६८||
verses.translation_en: For one who explains this supreme secret to the devotees, pure devotional service is guaranteed, and at the end he will come back to Me.
verses.translation_hi: जो इस परम गुह्य को मेरे भक्तों में सुनाएगा — मुझमें परम भक्ति करके वह असंशय मुझे ही आएगा।
verses.meaning_en: 'Ya imam paramam guhyam mad-bhakteshv abhidhasyati' — whoever will teach (abhidhasyati — explain, transmit) this supreme secret (paramam guhyam) among My devotees (mad-bhakteshu). 'Bhaktim mayi param kritva mam evai-shyat y asanshayah' — having performed supreme devotion (param bhakti) to Me, he will certainly (asanshayah — without doubt, beyond any uncertainty) come to Me. The teacher of the Gita receives the same promise as the one who surrenders to the Divine: that person too will come to the Divine. The transmission of this teaching is itself an act of supreme devotion. Teaching the Gita to those who are ready and willing to receive it is as sacred as any of the practices described in the eighteen chapters — it is the offering of the highest gift.
verses.meaning_hi: 'य इमं परमं गुह्यं मद्भक्तेष्वभिधास्यति' — जो इस परम गुह्य को मेरे भक्तों में सुनाएगा। 'भक्तिं मयि परां कृत्वा मामेवैष्यत्यसंशयः' — परम भक्ति करके असंशय मुझे आएगा। गीता के शिक्षक को भी वही वादा: जो समर्पण करने वाले को। इस शिक्षा का प्रसारण परम भक्ति का कार्य है।

verses.sanskrit:
न च तस्मान्मनुष्येषु कश्चिन्मे प्रियकृत्तमः |
भविता न च मे तस्मादन्यः प्रियतरो भुवि ||६९||
verses.translation_en: There is no servant in this world more dear to Me than he, nor will there ever be one more dear.
verses.translation_hi: मनुष्यों में उससे बढ़कर मेरा प्रिय करने वाला कोई नहीं है और भविष्य में भी पृथ्वी पर उससे अधिक प्रिय कोई नहीं होगा।
verses.meaning_en: 'Na ca tasmanmanushyeshu kashcin me priya-krit-tamah' — among human beings there is no one who does what is most dear to Me (priya-krit-tamah — the supreme doer of the dear, the one who does what pleases Me most) more than that person. 'Bhavita na ca me tasmad anyah priyataro bhuvi' — nor will there be anyone more dear (priyataro) to Me in the world than that person. The Divine declares: there is no greater service, no more beloved action, than the teaching of this Gita to receptive souls. The one who transmits this wisdom is the most beloved of all human beings to the Divine — more beloved than any other form of service, any other form of worship. This is the Gita's final, extraordinary valuation of the act of teaching.
verses.meaning_hi: 'न च तस्मान्मनुष्येषु कश्चिन्मे प्रियकृत्तमः' — मनुष्यों में उससे अधिक मेरा प्रिय करने वाला कोई नहीं। 'भविता न च मे तस्मादन्यः प्रियतरो भुवि' — भविष्य में भी अधिक प्रिय कोई नहीं होगा। दिव्य घोषणा: इस गीता का ग्राही आत्माओं को शिक्षण — दिव्य को सर्वाधिक प्रिय। यह शिक्षण के कार्य का गीता का अंतिम, असाधारण मूल्यांकन है।

verses.sanskrit:
अध्येष्यते च य इमं धर्म्यं संवादमावयोः |
ज्ञानयज्ञेन तेनाहमिष्टः स्यामिति मे मतिः ||७०||
verses.translation_en: And I declare that he who studies this sacred conversation of ours worships Me by his intelligence.
verses.translation_hi: और जो हम दोनों के इस धर्मय्य संवाद का अध्ययन करेगा — मेरी मति है कि वह ज्ञानयज्ञ से मुझे पूजेगा।
verses.meaning_en: 'Adhyeshyate ca ya imam dharmyam samvadam avayoh' — and whoever will study (adhyeshyate — read, study, recite) this righteous (dharmyam — in accordance with dharma, sacred) conversation of ours (avayoh — the dialogue between Me and Arjuna). 'Jnana-yajnena tenaham ishtah syam iti me matih' — by that person I will be worshipped (ishtah syam) by the sacrifice of knowledge (jnana-yajna) — thus is My conviction. The one who reads the Gita is performing jnana-yajna: the sacrifice of knowledge, the offering of the intellect to the fire of wisdom. Every sincere reading, every serious study, every genuine contemplation of the Gita's teaching is an act of sacred worship — as powerful and as direct as the most elaborate ritual sacrifice.
verses.meaning_hi: 'अध्येष्यते च य इमं धर्म्यं संवादमावयोः' — जो हम दोनों के इस धर्मय्य संवाद का अध्ययन करेगा। 'ज्ञानयज्ञेन तेनाहमिष्टः स्यामिति मे मतिः' — ज्ञानयज्ञ से मुझे पूजेगा। गीता का पाठक ज्ञानयज्ञ करता है: ज्ञान का यज्ञ, ज्ञान की अग्नि को बुद्धि का अर्पण। प्रत्येक ईमानदार पठन, प्रत्येक गहन चिंतन पवित्र पूजा का कार्य है।

verses.sanskrit:
श्रद्धावाननसूयश्च शृणुयादपि यो नरः |
सोऽपि मुक्तः शुभाँल्लोकान्प्राप्नुयात्पुण्यकर्मणाम् ||७१||
verses.translation_en: And one who listens with faith and without envy becomes free from sinful reactions and attains to the auspicious planets where the pious dwell.
verses.translation_hi: जो श्रद्धावान और अनसूयु मनुष्य इसे सुनेगा — वह भी मुक्त होकर पुण्यकर्माओं के शुभ लोकों को प्राप्त होगा।
verses.meaning_en: 'Shraddhavan anastuyash ca shrinuyad api yo narah' — even the person (yo narah — whatever human being) who simply listens (shrinuyad api — even if only hears) with faith (shraddhavan) and without envy (anastuyash — without malice, without spite, without the contaminating filter of envy). 'So'pi muktah shubhan lokan prapnuyat punya-karmanam' — that person too (so'pi — even that one) becomes liberated (muktah) and attains the auspicious worlds (shubhan lokan) of those who have performed righteous deeds (punya-karmanam). The 'api' (even, also) in this verse is the Gita's characteristic inclusion: even the one who does not teach, who cannot study, who can only listen with openness and faith — even that person receives the full fruit of the teaching. No sincere contact with this wisdom goes unrewarded.
verses.meaning_hi: 'श्रद्धावाननसूयश्च शृणुयादपि यो नरः' — श्रद्धावान और अनसूयु होकर सुनेगा। 'सोऽपि मुक्तः शुभाँल्लोकान्प्राप्नुयात्पुण्यकर्मणाम्' — वह भी मुक्त होकर शुभ लोक पाएगा। 'अपि' (भी) गीता की विशिष्ट समावेशिता है: जो केवल खुलेपन और श्रद्धा के साथ सुन सकता है — वह भी शिक्षा का पूर्ण फल पाता है।

verses.sanskrit:
कच्चिदेतच्छ्रुतं पार्थ त्वयैकाग्रेण चेतसा |
कच्चिदज्ञानसम्मोहः प्रनष्टस्ते धनञ्जय ||७२||
verses.translation_en: O conqueror of wealth, O son of Pritha, do you hear this attentively? And are your ignorance and illusions now dispelled?
verses.translation_hi: हे पार्थ! क्या यह एकाग्र चित्त से सुना? हे धनञ्जय! क्या अज्ञानसम्मोह प्रनष्ट हुआ?
verses.meaning_en: 'Kaccid etac chrutam Partha tvayaikagrenacetasa' — has this been heard by you, O Partha, with a one-pointed mind (ekagra-cetasa — with concentrated, undivided attention)? 'Kaccid ajnana-sammohah pranashtas te Dhananjaya' — has your confusion born of ignorance (ajnana-sammoha — the bewilderment arising from not-knowing) been destroyed (pranashta — completely dispelled, utterly removed), O Dhananjaya? Krishna pauses before the conversation ends to inquire with genuine care: did you hear this? Was your full attention present? Has the confusion that gripped you at the beginning of this dialogue — the confusion that made you drop your bow and refuse to fight — has that confusion been fully dissolved? The question is both practical and tender.
verses.meaning_hi: 'कच्चिदेतच्छ्रुतं पार्थ त्वयैकाग्रेण चेतसा' — हे पार्थ! क्या यह एकाग्र चित्त से सुना? 'कच्चिदज्ञानसम्मोहः प्रनष्टस्ते धनञ्जय' — हे धनञ्जय! अज्ञानसम्मोह प्रनष्ट हुआ? कृष्ण वास्तविक देखभाल के साथ पूछते हैं: क्या तुमने सुना? क्या भ्रम — जिसने अध्याय 1 में तुम्हारा धनुष गिरवाया — पूरी तरह विघटित हुआ? प्रश्न व्यावहारिक और कोमल दोनों है।

verses.sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच |
नष्टो मोहः स्मृतिर्लब्धा त्वत्प्रसादान्मयाच्युत |
स्थितोऽस्मि गतसन्देहः करिष्ये वचनं तव ||७३||
verses.translation_en: Arjuna said: My dear Krishna, O infallible one, my illusion is now gone. I have regained my memory by Your mercy. I am now firm and free from doubt and am prepared to act according to Your instructions.
verses.translation_hi: अर्जुन बोले: हे अच्युत! तुम्हारी कृपा से मेरा मोह नष्ट हो गया और स्मृति लौट आई। मैं गतसंदेह स्थित हूँ — तुम्हारा वचन करूँगा।
verses.meaning_en: 'Nashto mohah smritir labdha tvat-prasadan mayacyuta' — my delusion (mohah) has been destroyed (nashta), my memory (smritih — the memory of what I truly am, the Self's own recognition of itself) has been regained (labdha) by Your grace (tvat-prasat), O Achyuta (the unslipping one, the infallible). 'Sthito'smi gata-sandehah karishye vacanam tava' — I stand firm (sthitah asmi — I am established, I am grounded), free from all doubt (gata-sandehah — doubt has departed), and I will do Your word (karishye vacanam tava — I will carry out Your instruction). Arjuna's response is complete and clear: three transformations confirmed — the delusion is gone, the memory is restored, the doubt has departed. And one resolve: I will act according to Your word.
